Note 1 — Identification of person under surveillance Note 1 — Identification of person under surveillance The person under surveillance may be identified by a numerical reference identifying the relevant law enforcement agency and the year in which the application is made.

For example —WAPS 1/1999 (for the first application in 1999 by a member of the WA police force) The person under surveillance may be identified by a numerical reference identifying the relevant law enforcement agency and the year in which the application is made.

For example —WAPS 1/1999 (for the first application in 1999 by a member of the WA police force) Note 2 — Lodging of Application for warrant Note 2 — Lodging of Application for warrant The application is to be lodged with the magistrate.

An application must be accompanied by —

• an “Authorisation of surveillance”; and

• an affidavit containing a “Statement of facts in support of application for warrant”

in accordance with Surveillance Devices Act 1998, s. 15(3)(a), (b), (c) and (f).

An application should also be accompanied by a draft warrant in the form of the warrant being sought. The application is to be lodged with the magistrate.

An application must be accompanied by —

• an “Authorisation of surveillance”; and

• an affidavit containing a “Statement of facts in support of application for warrant”

in accordance with Surveillance Devices Act 1998, s. 15(3)(a), (b), (c) and (f).

An application should also be accompanied by a draft warrant in the form of the warrant being sought.

sections.h:
Please provide an outline of the investigative strategy, including what actions investigators have done, may or intend to do. Where listening devices are requested, please include details of the proposed strategy which will assist in generating conversations between the target(s) and others regarding the offence(s) under investigation.

Please provide the extent to which evidence or information is likely to be obtained by methods of investigation not involving the use of a surveillance device and the intelligence value and the evidentiary value of any information sought to be obtained.

(e.g. consider making an application for a warrant(s) under the Telecommunications (Interception and Access) Act 1979 (Cth), obtain a controlled operation under the Criminal Investigation (Covert Powers) Act 2012, conduct covert physical surveillance, collate and manage information provided by human/community sources, arrest and interview target(s), identify and obtain victim/witness statements, identify and obtain any relevant CCTV footage, media release etc.

(e.g. consider making an application for a warrant(s) under the Telecommunications (Interception and Access) Act 1979 (Cth), obtain a controlled operation under the Criminal Investigation (Covert Powers) Act 2012, conduct covert physical surveillance, collate and manage information provided by human/community sources, arrest and interview target(s), identify and obtain victim/witness statements, identify and obtain any relevant CCTV footage, media release etc.

sections.h:
This form is to be completed by any Investigating Officer who wishes to communicate protected information, as defined by section 45 of the Surveillance Devices Act 2004 (Cth) (the Act), to any officer of a law enforcement agency other than Western Australia Police.

It is the policy of Special Operations Application Unit (SOAU) not to permit communication of protected information obtained through the use of surveillance devices with other law enforcement agencies without the permission of the Officer-in-Charge of SOAU. The use, communication and publication of protected information is restricted under the Act and is subject to record keeping requirements if communication of the protected information is approved by the OIC SOAU.

The Investigating Officer is to complete the below form and submit to SOAU for approval.

sections.h:
Section 45(7) of the Surveillance Devices Act 2004 (Cth) If protected information obtained through the use of a surveillance device by a law enforcement officer of a particular law enforcement agency (the originating agency):

is communicated to another law enforcement agency (by communicating it to the chief officer or another officer of that agency); or is communicated to any agency that is not a law enforcement agency (other than the Australian Security Intelligence Organisation and the agencies within the meaning of the Intelligence Services Act 2001) (by communicating it to the officer in charge of that agency or to another officer of that agency);

for a particular purpose, the protected information that has been so communicated:

may be communicated from one officer to another within that agency or organisation for that purpose only; and must not, except for the purpose of bringing a relevant proceeding, or a State or Territory relevant proceeding, be communicated to any person who is not a member of that agency or organisation.

sections.h:
Commonwealth SDA (Warrant approved/issued)

Your application for a Commonwealth SDA warrant(s)/or extension to warrant XX of 202X, has gone to court and a warrant(s) was issued/or extension granted by [TITLE – Justice] XXXXX for a period of XX days.

Please note the EXPIRY DATE of XX/XX/202X.

It is your responsibility to submit a supplementary TRF to the State Intelligence and command Portfolio, Covert Coordinator 14 days prior to the expiry date if you believe that you are going to require an extension/further extension.

Please liaise with the Police Technical Unit, Operations Manager on 08 9315 4409 (all hours) to arrange the installation or retrieval of surveillance devices pursuant to your warrant (s).

As the applicant for a Commonwealth SDA you must be aware and abide by the following legislation compliance matters:

For assistance on Commonwealth SDA procedures seek further information:

Blue Book | State Crime | Crime Support and Services | Special Operations Application Unit | Surveillance Devices – PROCEDURES Execution of Warrant:

Should you as the applicant cease for any reason to have responsibility for the execution of the warrant, the Chief Officer of the agency in writing, must nominate another person as the law enforcement officer. If this is unable to be done, the warrant should be revoked and another warrant sought.

(e.g. transfer to another business unit, extended leave etc.) See s6(3) Surveillance Devices Act 2004 (Commonwealth)

Storage of Protected Information:

Section 46(1)(a) of the Act requires the chief officer to ensure that every record comprising protected information is kept in a secure place that is not accessible to people who are not entitled to deal with the information. The definition of 'protected information' under s 44(1) of the Act includes information obtained from the use of a surveillance device.

PLEASE ENSURE THAT ANY STORAGE OF PRODUCT ON USB’s, HARD DRIVES OR DISCS ARE ENCRYPTED AND ONLY AVAILABLE TO THOSE PERSON ENTITLED TO DEAL WITH IT.

Keeping Records of Each use:

Under s 45 of the Act, the use, recording, communication, or publication of protected information is prohibited unless it is for a permitted purpose detailed under s 45(4) of the Act. Section 45(5) of the Act further provides permitted purposes to use, record, communicate or publish, or admit in evidence protected information.

Under s 52(1)(e) of the Act, the chief officer of a law enforcement agency must keep 'details of each use' by the agency, or by a law enforcement officer of the agency, of information obtained using a surveillance device or access to data held in a computer. Section 52(1)(f) of the Act requires the same for ‘details of each communication' by a law enforcement officer of the agency to a person other than a law enforcement officer of the agency.

PLEASE ENSURE RECORDS ARE KEPT ON EACH USE AND THE RECORD KEEPING REQUIREMENTS SURROUNDING EACH USE. PLEASE KEEP DETAILED RUNNING SHEETS ON EACH USE OF THE SDA AND SUPPLY WITH THE COMPLETE UCL WITH A COPY OF THESE RUNNING SHEETS TO BE KEPT ON FILE FOR INSPECTION BY THE COMMONWEALTH OMBUDSMAN.

Revocation of Warrants:

The chief officer must revoke a surveillance device warrant, by instrument in writing, if the prescribed circumstances in s 21 of the Act apply. This includes where the chief officer is satisfied that the use of a surveillance device under the warrant is no longer necessary for the purpose of enabling evidence to be obtained of the commission of a relevant offence or the identity or location of an offender.

PLEASE NOTIFY SOAU IMMEDIATELY IF THE WAARANT IS NO LONGER NECESSARY FOR ITS ORIGINAL PURPOSE. SOAU WILL THEN ARRANGE REVOCATION WITH THE CHIEF OFFICER.

Final Investigators Report:

Section 49(1) of the Act states that the chief officer must as soon as practicable after a warrant or authority ceases to be in force, make a report to the Minister.

PLEASE REFERENCE RECORDS KEPT FOR THE USE OF THE SDA INCLUDING ANY RUNNING SHEETS WHEN COMPILING THE INVESTIGATOR REPORT.

Please ensure all appropriate details are included within the Final Investigators Report addressing all matters as per section 49(2)(b) of the Act. The report must include details of any premises on which the device was installed or any place at which the device was used.

For assistance in completing this report please refer to example report situated at:

Blue Book | State Crime | Crime Support and Services | Special Operations Application Unit | Surveillance Devices | (SDA) – Commonwealth Procedures Forms NOTE - If you are making an application for any covert services, either for a telecommunication intercept, surveillance devices, physical surveillance or undercover officers you are required to complete a 'WAPOL Tasking Request Form’ Please contact the SOAU Covert Applications Supervisor on 9223 3521 (ext 13521) if you have any queries or concerns.

Destruction of Protected Information:

Destruction of Protected Information and/or data obtained through the execution of surveillance device warrant will be undertaken in accordance to a destruction regime pursuant to section 46(1)(b) of the Act.

Only upon issue of a Destruction Order is Protected Information to be destroyed.

Inquiries regarding the destruction of Protected Information should be directed to SOAU.

Any Protected Information obtained through execution of a surveillance warrant held by another law enforcement or government agency shall be subject to the WA Police Force Destruction Process with evidence of the destruction required to be recorded within the warrant file.

SOAU will manage and coordinate the destruction of all protected information on behalf of the WA Police Force.

sections.h:
C O N F I D E N T I A L

Officer In Charge

Special Operations Application Unit

6th Floor,

, W.A. 6000

SURVEILLANCE DEVICES ACT 2004 (Cth) - SECT 49 EXAMPLE

Report on each warrant or authorisation

This report refers to the following Surveillance Devices Act (2004) (Cth) warrant or authorisation:

Operation: OPERATION NAME

Warrant/Authorisation Number: WARRANT NUMBER

Applicant: APPLICANT LISTED ON THE WARRANT

(2) In the case of a surveillance device warrant, or an emergency authorisation for the use of a surveillance device, or a tracking device authorisation, the report must:

(a) state whether the warrant or authorisation was executed; YES OR NO, IF NO, WAS THE WARRANT REVOKED AND IF NOT PLEASE STATE THE REASON

(b) if so:

(i) state the name of the person primarily responsible for the execution of the warrant or authorisation; SELF EXPLANATORY (SE)

(ii) state the name of each person involved in the installation, maintenance or retrieval of the surveillance device; IF APPLICABLE PLEASE OBTAIN DETAILS FROM POLICE TECHNICAL UNIT INCLUDING OBTAINING ANY RELEVANT RUNNING SHEETS, TIMES, DATES, ETC OF EACH INSTALLATION, MAINTENANCE INCLUDING ANY ATTEMPTS MADE.

(iii) state the kind of surveillance device used; LISTENING, OPTICAL, TRACKING AND/OR DATA

(iv) state the period during which the device was used; INCLUDE EACH TIME THE SD WAS DEPLOYED, INCLUDING TIMES, DATES, LOCATIONS (PLEASE PROVIDE RUNNING SHEETS)

(v) state the name, if known, of any person whose conversations or activities were overheard, recorded, monitored, listened to or observed by the use of the device; REFER TO RELEVANT TIMES AND DATES LISTED AT POINT (iv). PLEASE DESCRIBE THE DEVICE/EQUIPMENT THE MATERIAL WAS RECORDED ON, DESCRIBE THE RELEVANT SECURITY AND ENCRYPTION.

(vi) state the name, if known, of any person whose location was determined by the use of a tracking device; REFER TO RELEVANT LOCATIONS, TIMES AND DATES LISTED AT POINT (iv)

(vii) give details of any premises on which the device was installed or any place at which the device was used; SE

(viii) give details of any object in or on which the device was installed and any premises where the object was located when the device was installed; SE

(ix) if the warrant is issued or the authorisation given in respect of the investigation of a relevant offence--give details of the benefit to the investigation of the use of the device and of the general use made or to be made of any evidence or information obtained by the use of the device; SE

(x) if the warrant is issued or the authorisation given in respect of the location and safe recovery of a child to whom a recovery order relates--give details of use of the device in assisting with the location and safe recovery of the child; INCLUDE EACH TIME THE SD WAS DEPLOYED, INCLUDING TIMES, DATES, LOCATIONS (PLEASE PROVIDE RUNNING SHEETS

(xa) if the warrant is issued or the authorisation given for the purposes of an integrity operation--give details of the benefit to the operation of the use of the device and of the general use made or to be made of any evidence or information obtained by the use of the device; SE

(xb) if the warrant is a control order warrant--give the details specified in subsection (2A); NOT APPLICABLE

(xi) give details of the communication of evidence or information obtained by the use of the device to persons other than officers of the agency; IF YES PLEASE PROVIDE DETAILS, NOTES, RUNNING SHEETS ETC.

(xii) give details of the compliance with the conditions (if any) to which the warrant or authorisation was subject; GIVE DETAILS IF APPLICABLE

(c) if the warrant or authorisation was extended or varied, state:

(i) the number of extensions or variations; SE

(ii) the reasons for them. GIVE DETAILS

(2A) For the purposes of subparagraph (2)(b)(xb), the details are:

(a) the benefit of the use of the device in: NA

(i) protecting the public from a terrorist act; NA

(ii) preventing the provision of support for, or the facilitation of, a terrorist act; NA

(iii) preventing the provision of support for, or the facilitation of, the engagement in a hostile activity in a foreign country; NA

(iv) determining whether a control order has been, or is being, complied with; NA

(b) the general use to be made of any evidence or information obtained by the use of the device. NA

(2B) In the case of a computer access warrant, or an emergency authorisation, for access to data held in a computer, the report must:

(a) state whether the warrant or authorisation was executed; YES OR NO, IF NO, WAS THE WARRANT REVOKED AND IF NOT PLEASE STATE THE REASON

(b) if so:

(i) state the name of the person primarily responsible for the execution of the warrant or authorisation; SE

(ii) state the name of each person involved in accessing data under the warrant or authorisation; SE

(iii) state the period during which the data was accessed; PLEASE PROVIDE TIMES, DATES AND RUNNING SHEETS

(iv) state the name, if known, of any person whose data was accessed; IF APPLICABLE PLEASE OBTAIN DETAILS FROM COMPUTER CRIME, POLICE TECHNICAL UNIT OR SIMILIAR INCLUDING OBTAINING ANY RELEVANT RUNNING SHEETS, TIMES, DATES, ETC

(v) give details of any premises at which the computer was located; REFER TO RELEVANT LOCATIONS, TIMES AND DATES

(vi) if the warrant is issued, or the authorisation is given, in respect of the investigation of a relevant offence--give details of the benefit to the investigation of the accessed data and of the general use made, or to be made, of any evidence or information obtained by the access to data; SE

(vii) if the warrant is issued, or the authorisation is given, in respect of the location and safe recovery of a child to whom a recovery order relates--give details of the use of the accessed data in assisting with the location and safe recovery of the child; SE

(viii) if the warrant is issued, or the authorisation is given, for the purposes of an integrity operation--give details of the benefit to the operation of the accessed data and of the general use made, or to be made, of any evidence or information obtained by the access to data; SE

(ix) if the warrant is a control order access warrant--give the details specified in subsection (2C); NA, WAPF DOES NOT APPLY FOR CONTROL ORDER WARRANTS

(x) give details of the communication of evidence or information obtained by access to data held in the computer to persons other than officers of the agency; NA

(xi) give details of the compliance with the conditions (if any) to which the warrant or authorisation was subject; NA

(c) if the warrant or authorisation was extended or varied, state:

(i) the number of extensions or variations; NA

(ii) the reasons for them. NA

(2C) For the purposes of subparagraph (2B)(b)(ix), the details are:

(a) the benefit of obtaining access to data held in the computer in:

(i) protecting the public from a terrorist act; NA

(ii) preventing the provision of support for, or the facilitation of, a terrorist act; NA

(iii) preventing the provision of support for, or the facilitation of, the engagement in a hostile activity in a foreign country; NA

(iv) determining whether a control order has been, or is being, complied with; NA

(b) the general use to be made of any evidence or information obtained by access to data held in the computer. NA

(3) In the case of a retrieval warrant, the report must:

(a) give details of any premises entered, anything opened and any object removed and replaced under the warrant; PLEASE PROVIDE TIMES, DATES AND RUNNING SHEETS

(b) state whether the surveillance device was retrieved under the warrant; PLEASE PROVIDE TIMES, DATES AND RUNNING SHEETS

(c) if the device was not retrieved, state the reason why; IF NO, WAS THE WARRANT REVOKED AND IF NOT PLEASE STATE THE REASON

(d) give details of the compliance with the conditions (if any) to which the warrant was subject. SE

Forwarded for information and reporting.

SURVEILLANCE DEVICES ACT 2004 (Cth) - SECT 49

Report on each warrant or authorisation.

This report refers to the following Surveillance Devices Act (2004) (Cth) warrant or authorisation:

Operation:

Warrant/Authorisation Number:

Applicant:

(2) In the case of a surveillance device warrant, or an emergency authorisation for the use of a surveillance device, or a tracking device authorisation, the report must:

(a) state whether the warrant or authorisation was executed;

(b) if so:

(i) state the name of the person primarily responsible for the execution of the warrant or authorisation;

(ii) state the name of each person involved in the installation, maintenance or retrieval of the surveillance device;

(iii) state the kind of surveillance device used;

(iv) state the period during which the device was used;

(v) state the name, if known, of any person whose conversations or activities were overheard, recorded, monitored, listened to or observed by the use of the device;

(vi) state the name, if known, of any person whose location was determined by the use of a tracking device;

(vii) give details of any premises on which the device was installed or any place at which the device was used;

(viii) give details of any object in or on which the device was installed and any premises where the object was located when the device was installed;

(ix) if the warrant is issued or the authorisation given in respect of the investigation of a relevant offence--give details of the benefit to the investigation of the use of the device and of the general use made or to be made of any evidence or information obtained by the use of the device;

(x) if the warrant is issued or the authorisation given in respect of the location and safe recovery of a child to whom a recovery order relates--give details of use of the device in assisting with the location and safe recovery of the child;

(xa) if the warrant is issued or the authorisation given for the purposes of an integrity operation--give details of the benefit to the operation of the use of the device and of the general use made or to be made of any evidence or information obtained by the use of the device;

(xb) if the warrant is a control order warrant--give the details specified in subsection (2A);

(xi) give details of the communication of evidence or information obtained by the use of the device to persons other than officers of the agency;

(xii) give details of the compliance with the conditions (if any) to which the warrant or authorisation was subject;

(c) if the warrant or authorisation was extended or varied, state:

(i) the number of extensions or variations;

(ii) the reasons for them.

(2A) For the purposes of subparagraph (2)(b)(xb), the details are:

(a) the benefit of the use of the device in:

(i) protecting the public from a terrorist act;

(ii) preventing the provision of support for, or the facilitation of, a terrorist act;

(iii) preventing the provision of support for, or the facilitation of, the engagement in a hostile activity in a foreign country;

(iv) determining whether a control order has been, or is being, complied with;

(b) the general use to be made of any evidence or information obtained by the use of the device.

(2B) In the case of a computer access warrant, or an emergency authorisation, for access to data held in a computer, the report must:

(a) state whether the warrant or authorisation was executed;

(b) if so:

(i) state the name of the person primarily responsible for the execution of the warrant or authorisation;

(ii) state the name of each person involved in accessing data under the warrant or authorisation;

(iii) state the period during which the data was accessed;

(iv) state the name, if known, of any person whose data was accessed;

(v) give details of any premises at which the computer was located;

(vi) if the warrant is issued, or the authorisation is given, in respect of the investigation of a relevant offence--give details of the benefit to the investigation of the accessed data and of the general use made, or to be made, of any evidence or information obtained by the access to data;

(vii) if the warrant is issued, or the authorisation is given, in respect of the location and safe recovery of a child to whom a recovery order relates--give details of the use of the accessed data in assisting with the location and safe recovery of the child;

(viii) if the warrant is issued, or the authorisation is given, for the purposes of an integrity operation--give details of the benefit to the operation of the accessed data and of the general use made, or to be made, of any evidence or information obtained by the access to data;

(ix) if the warrant is a control order access warrant--give the details specified in subsection (2C);

(x) give details of the communication of evidence or information obtained by access to data held in the computer to persons other than officers of the agency;

(xi) give details of the compliance with the conditions (if any) to which the warrant or authorisation was subject;

(c) if the warrant or authorisation was extended or varied, state:

(i) the number of extensions or variations;

(ii) the reasons for them.

(2C) For the purposes of subparagraph (2B)(b)(ix), the details are:

(a) the benefit of obtaining access to data held in the computer in:

(i) protecting the public from a terrorist act;

(ii) preventing the provision of support for, or the facilitation of, a terrorist act;

(iii) preventing the provision of support for, or the facilitation of, the engagement in a hostile activity in a foreign country;

(iv) determining whether a control order has been, or is being, complied with;

(b) the general use to be made of any evidence or information obtained by access to data held in the computer.

(3) In the case of a retrieval warrant, the report must:

(a) give details of any premises entered, anything opened and any object removed and replaced under the warrant;

(b) state whether the surveillance device was retrieved under the warrant;

(c) if the device was not retrieved, state the reason why;

(d) give details of the compliance with the conditions (if any) to which the warrant was subject.

Forwarded for information and reporting.

sections.h:
Keeping Records of Each use:

Under s 45 of the Act, the use, recording, communication, or publication of protected information is prohibited unless it is for a permitted purpose detailed under s 45(4) of the Act. Section 45(5) of the Act further provides permitted purposes to use, record, communicate or publish, or admit in evidence protected information.

Under s 52(1)(e) of the Act, the chief officer of a law enforcement agency must keep 'details of each use' by the agency, or by a law enforcement officer of the agency, of information obtained using a surveillance device or access to data held in a computer. Section 52(1)(f) of the Act requires the same for ‘details of each communication' by a law enforcement officer of the agency to a person other than a law enforcement officer of the agency.

PLEASE ENSURE RECORDS ARE KEPT ON EACH USE AND THE RECORD KEEPING REQUIREMENTS SURROUNDING EACH USE. PLEASE KEEP DETAILED RUNNING SHEETS ON EACH USE OF THE SDA AND SUPPLY A COPY OF THESE RUNNING SHEETS ON SUBMISSION OF THIS UCLTO BE KEPT ON FILE AND MADE AVAILABLE TO THE COMMONWEALTH OMBUDSMAN.

Disclosure or Use:

sections.h:
Here are the key steps in the (SDA) Emergency Authorisation Process under the Surveillance Devices Act 1998 (s 21):

Annexure 1. Essential checklist for Emergency Use of Surveillance Devices.

Annexure 2. Application for Emergency Authorisation

Annexure 3. Emergency Authorisation

Annexure 4. Section 21 Surveillance Device Act 1998

✅ Essential Checklist for Emergency Use of Surveillance Devices

1. Imminent Threat or Serious Offence

Is there an imminent threat of serious violence to a person or substantial damage to property?

2. Drug Offence Criteria (if applicable)

Has an indictable drug offence (or external indictable drug offence) been, or is likely to be, committed?

Is the immediate use of a surveillance device necessary to:

Deal with the threat or investigate the offence?

Obtain evidence of the offence?

Identify or locate the offender?

Are the circumstances so serious and urgent that use of the device is warranted?

Is it not practicable to apply under section 15 or 16?

3. General Serious Offence Criteria (if applicable)

Has an offence punishable by 2 or more years’ imprisonment been, or is likely to be, committed?

Is the immediate use of a surveillance device necessary to:

Deal with the threat or investigate the offence?

Obtain evidence of the offence?

Identify or locate the offender?

Are the circumstances so serious and urgent that use of the device is warranted?

Is it not practicable to apply under section 15 or 16?

[Form amended: Act No. 74 of 2004 s. 73(3); Gazette 5 May 2006

[Form amended: Act No. 74 of 2004 s. 73(3) and (4); Gazette

Is the immediate use of a surveillance device necessary to: Deal with the threat or investigate the offence? Obtain evidence of the offence? Identify or locate the offender? Are the circumstances so serious and urgent that use of the device is warranted? Is it not practicable to apply under section 15 or 16? [Form amended: Act No. 74 of 2004 s. 73(3); Gazette 5 May 2006 [Form amended: Act No. 74 of 2004 s. 73(3) and (4); Gazette Surveillance Devices Act 1998

sections.h:
(1) An authorised person may issue an emergency authorisation under this section if satisfied by an application made in that behalf that there are reasonable grounds for believing —

(a) that an imminent threat of serious violence to a person or of substantial damage to property exists;

(b) that —

(i) an indictable drug offence or an external indictable drug offence has been or may have been, is being or is about to be, or is likely to be, committed;

(ii) the use of a listening device, an optical surveillance device, or a tracking device is immediately necessary for the purpose of dealing with that threat or investigating that offence or suspected offence, or of enabling evidence to be obtained of the commission of that offence, or the identity or location of the offender;

(iii) the circumstances are so serious and the matter is of such urgency that the use of the device in the course of the duty of the applicant is warranted; and

(iv) an application under section 15 or 16 is not practicable;

or

(c) that —

[(i) deleted]

(ia) an offence punishable by 2 or more year’s imprisonment has been or may have been, is being or is about to be, or is likely to be, committed;

(ii) the use of a listening device, an optical surveillance device, or a tracking device is immediately necessary for the purpose of dealing with that threat or investigating that offence or suspected offence, or of enabling evidence to be obtained of the commission of that offence, or the identity or location of the offender;

(iii) the circumstances are so serious and the matter is of such urgency that the use of the device in the course of the duty of the applicant is warranted; and

(iv) an application under section 15 or 16 is not practicable.

(2) An authorised person may issue an emergency authorisation under this section if satisfied by an application made in that behalf that there are reasonable grounds for believing that —

(a) retrieval of a listening device, an optical surveillance device, or a tracking device is immediately necessary in order that an investigation into an indictable drug offence or an external indictable drug offence is not jeopardized;

(b) the circumstances are so serious and the matter is of such urgency that the retrieval of the device in the course of the duty of the applicant is warranted; and

(c) an application under section 22 is not practicable.

(3) An application under subsection (1) or (2) may be made either orally or in writing or by means of a radio, telephone, video recording, facsimile transmission or any other communication device.

(4) A person to whom an emergency authorisation has been issued under subsection (1) must deliver without delay a written report to a judge —

(a) stating whether a surveillance device was used under the authorisation;

(b) if a surveillance device was so used —

(i) giving particulars of the use and the period during which it was used;

(ii) specifying the name, if known, of any person whose private conversation was monitored, recorded or listened to or whose private activity was observed or recorded;

(iii) specifying the name, if known, of any person whose geographical location was monitored by use of a tracking device;

(iv) containing particulars of any premises where the device was installed or any place at which the device was used;

(v) containing particulars of any specified object in or on which the device was installed or any premises where the object was located when the device was installed;

(vi) containing particulars of the general use made or to be made of any evidence or information obtained by use of the device; and

(vii) containing particulars of any previous use of a surveillance device in connection with the matter or offence or suspected offence in respect of which the emergency authorisation was granted.

(5) A person to whom an emergency authorisation has been issued under subsection (2) must deliver without delay a written report to a judge —

(a) stating whether a surveillance device was retrieved under the authorisation; and

(b) if a surveillance device was so retrieved, giving particulars of the retrieval.

(6) Subject to necessary modifications, the provisions of section 13(8)(f), (g), (i) and (j), (9), (11) and (12) apply to emergency authorisations in the same manner as they apply to warrants.

(7) An authorised person may issue one composite emergency authorisation having effect in accordance with its terms in respect of the attachment, installation, use or maintenance of more than one kind of surveillance device or a surveillance device that has more than one kind of function.

(8) Where a report is given to a judge under subsection (4), the judge may direct that any record of evidence or information obtained by the use of the surveillance device to which the report relates be brought before the judge.

(9) A record of evidence or information brought before a judge under subsection (8) must be kept in the custody of the Supreme Court and the judge may order that it be returned, or made available to any person, or destroyed.

(10) On consideration of a report under this section, a judge may direct that use of the surveillance device cease immediately.

[Section 21 amended: No. 78 of 2003 s. 74.]
sections.b: (1) An authorised person may issue an emergency authorisation under this section if satisfied by an application made in that behalf that there are reasonable grounds for believing — (a) that an imminent threat of serious violence to a person or of substantial damage to property exists; (b) that — (i) an indictable drug offence or an external indictable drug offence has been or may have been, is being or is about to be, or is likely to be, committed; (ii) the use of a listening device, an optical surveillance device, or a tracking device is immediately necessary for the purpose of dealing with that threat or investigating that offence or suspected offence, or of enabling evidence to be obtained of the commission of that offence, or the identity or location of the offender; (iii) the circumstances are so serious and the matter is of such urgency that the use of the device in the course of the duty of the applicant is warranted; and (iv) an application under section 15 or 16 is not practicable; or (c) that — [(i) deleted] (ia) an offence punishable by 2 or more year’s imprisonment has been or may have been, is being or is about to be, or is likely to be, committed; (ii) the use of a listening device, an optical surveillance device, or a tracking device is immediately necessary for the purpose of dealing with that threat or investigating that offence or suspected offence, or of enabling evidence to be obtained of the commission of that offence, or the identity or location of the offender; (iii) the circumstances are so serious and the matter is of such urgency that the use of the device in the course of the duty of the applicant is warranted; and (iv) an application under section 15 or 16 is not practicable. sections.h:
📲 1. Contact Covert Applications Team

• During business hours: Call 08 9223 3521.

• After hours: On-call SOAU located on 1st Contact within Teams / Hey Sarge.

🔍 2. Initial Consideration

• Assess the situation under section 21 of the SDA to determine if emergency authorisation is applicable (see checklist)

• Evaluate how this fits into your operational strategy.

👥 3. Internal Briefing

• Brief your Senior Investigating Officer or relevant supervisor.

• Obtain verbal endorsement before proceeding.

📞 4. Notify Technical Support

• Contact the Police Technical Unit (PTU) to inform them of the deployment.

• If after hours, use the on-call number 0439 912 095

• Confirm their capability to assist.

📲 5. SOAU Assessment

• SOAU on call to assess the evidence and plan to determine if this is an ‘Emergency Application’ or ‘Urgent Formal Request’ / progress contentious issues to Operations Manager and / or OIC SOAU.

🕒 6. Create TEAMS

• SOAU create a TEAMS chat, include I/O, SOAU on call, SOAU Operations Manager and OIC, Authorising officer (Insp).

• Progress to Assistant Commissioner (State Crime) via Staff Officer.

📝 7. Complete Documentation

• Provide grounds for application to on-call SOAU.

• Forward it to the nominated affidavit writer.

🗂️ 8. Document Preparation

• The Covert Applications Team prepares the Emergency Application

📅 9. Authorisation

• You must sign the application to present to the Assistant Commissioner.

• This may be done in front of the AC or remotely via Teams / e-mail dependant on availability.

📄 10. Formal Report Submission

• A report under s 21(4) & is drafted by the Covert Applications Team, without delay for the next available Court sitting.

• You must provide details of evidence obtained under emergency.

• Formal appointment made with Supreme Court Judge.

📲 1. Contact Covert Applications Team

• During business hours: Call 08 9223 3521.

• After hours: On-call SOAU located on 1st Contact within Teams / Hey Sarge.

🔍 2. Initial Consideration

• Assess the situation under section 21 of the SDA to determine if emergency authorisation is applicable (see checklist)

• Evaluate how this fits into your operational strategy.

👥 3. Internal Briefing

• Brief your Senior Investigating Officer or relevant supervisor.

• Obtain verbal endorsement before proceeding.

📞 4. Notify Technical Support

• Contact the Police Technical Unit (PTU) to inform them of the deployment.

• If after hours, use the on-call number 0439 912 095

• Confirm their capability to assist.

• SOAU on call to assess the evidence and plan to determine if this is an ‘Emergency Application’ or ‘Urgent Formal Request’ / progress contentious issues to Operations Manager and / or OIC SOAU.

🕒 6. Create TEAMS

• SOAU create a TEAMS chat, include I/O, SOAU on call, SOAU Operations Manager and OIC, Authorising officer (Insp).

• Progress to Assistant Commissioner (State Crime) via Staff Officer.

📝 7. Complete Documentation

• Provide grounds for application to on-call SOAU.

• Forward it to the nominated affidavit writer.

🗂️ 8. Document Preparation

• The Covert Applications Team prepares the Emergency Application

📅 9. Authorisation

• You must sign the application to present to the Assistant Commissioner.

• This may be done in front of the AC or remotely via Teams / e-mail dependant on availability.

📄 10. Formal Report Submission

• A report under s 21(4) & is drafted by the Covert Applications Team, without delay for the next available Court sitting.

• You must provide details of evidence obtained under emergency.

• Formal appointment made with Supreme Court Judge.

Formal Report Submission • A report under s 21(4) & is drafted by the Covert Applications Team, without delay for the next available Court sitting. • You must provide details of evidence obtained under emergency. • Formal appointment made with Supreme Court Judge. 📤11. Retrospective Tasking • Submit a Tasking Request Form (TRF) via IDM for services rendered by SOAU and PTU. 📲 1. Contact Covert Applications Team • During business hours: Call 08 9223 3521. • After hours: On-call SOAU located on 1st Contact within Teams / Hey Sarge. 🔍 2. Initial Consideration • Assess the situation under section 21 of the SDA to determine if emergency authorisation is applicable (see checklist) • Evaluate how this fits into your operational strategy. 👥 3. Internal Briefing • Brief your Senior Investigating Officer or relevant supervisor. • Obtain verbal endorsement before proceeding. 📞 4. Notify Technical Support • Contact the Police Technical Unit (PTU) to inform them of the deployment. • If after hours, use the on-call number 0439 912 095 • Confirm their capability to assist. 📲 5. SOAU Assessment • SOAU on call to assess the evidence and plan to determine if this is an ‘Emergency Application’ or ‘Urgent Formal Request’ / progress contentious issues to Operations Manager and / or OIC SOAU. 🕒 6. Create TEAMS • SOAU create a TEAMS chat, include I/O, SOAU on call, SOAU Operations Manager and OIC, Authorising officer (Insp). • Progress to Assistant Commissioner (State Crime) via Staff Officer. 📝 7. Complete Documentation • Provide grounds for application to on-call SOAU. • Forward it to the nominated affidavit writer. 🗂️ 8. Create TEAMS • SOAU create a TEAMS chat, include I/O, SOAU on call, SOAU Operations Manager and OIC, Authorising officer (Insp). • Progress to Assistant Commissioner (State Crime) via Staff Officer. 📝 7. Complete Documentation • Provide grounds for application to on-call SOAU. • Forward it to the nominated affidavit writer. 🗂️ 8. Document Preparation • The Covert Applications Team prepares the Emergency Application 📅 9. Authorisation • You must sign the application to present to the Assistant Commissioner. • This may be done in front of the AC or remotely via Teams / e-mail dependant on availability. 📄 10. Formal Report Submission • A report under s 21(4) & is drafted by the Covert Applications Team, without delay for the next available Court sitting. • You must provide details of evidence obtained under emergency. • Formal appointment made with Supreme Court Judge. 📤11. sections.h:
Version Control

TABLE OF CONTENTS
sections.b: Version Control TABLE OF CONTENTS

sections.h:
The following procedures are provided for use by all Western Australia Police Force personnel exercising powers provided under the Surveillance Devices Act 2004 (Commonwealth) (the Act). The procedures relate to the application processes, storage, use and communication, destruction and other considerations when using the Act.

The procedures also contain information and guidance provided to the WA Police Force by the Commonwealth Ombudsman.

These procedures should be read in conjunction with the Act and are provided to maintain and ensure legislative compliance with the Act, by the WA Police Force.
sections.b: The following procedures are provided for use by all Western Australia Police Force personnel exercising powers provided under the Surveillance Devices Act 2004 (Commonwealth) (the Act). The procedures relate to the application processes, storage, use and communication, destruction and other considerations when using the Act. The procedures also contain information and guidance provided to the WA Police Force by the Commonwealth Ombudsman. These procedures should be read in conjunction with the Act and are provided to maintain and ensure legislative compliance with the Act, by the WA Police Force.

sections.h: Various positions and personnel within the WA Police Force are required to undertake certain roles and maintain specific responsibilities within the Act. Following is a brief outline of these positions and roles.
sections.b: Various positions and personnel within the WA Police Force are required to undertake certain roles and maintain specific responsibilities within the Act. Following is a brief outline of these positions and roles.

sections.h:
The Chief Officer has numerous powers and functions afforded to him/her under the Act. These include:

Section 20(2) Warrant revocation;

Section 46(1)(b) Destruction of records;

Section 49(1) Warrant and authorisation reporting; and Section 50(1) Annual report to the Minister.

The powers and functions afforded to the Chief Officer under the Act may be delegated pursuant to section 63 of the Act to a suitable approving officer within the WA Police Force. Currently, the Commissioners functions are delegated to the Assistant Commissioner (State Crime) and Commander (State Crime).

The Officer-in-Charge (OIC) Special Operations Applications Unit (SOAU) holds copies of delegations, and they are also available from the Corporate Knowledge Base under “Legislative Instruments.”
sections.b: The Chief Officer has numerous powers and functions afforded to him/her under the Act. These include: Section 20(2) Warrant revocation; Section 46(1)(b) Destruction of records; Section 49(1) Warrant and authorisation reporting; and Section 50(1) Annual report to the Minister. The powers and functions afforded to the Chief Officer under the Act may be delegated pursuant to section 63 of the Act to a suitable approving officer within the WA Police Force. Currently, the Commissioners functions are delegated to the Assistant Commissioner (State Crime) and Commander (State Crime). The Officer-in-Charge (OIC) Special Operations Applications Unit (SOAU) holds copies of delegations, and they are also available from the Corporate Knowledge Base under “Legislative Instruments.”

sections.h:
The applicant will be ultimately responsible for a warrant or power exercised under the provisions of the Act. The applicant based upon his or her knowledge initially seeks approval for a warrant to be obtained under the provisions of the Act. An applicant is encouraged to consult with the SOAU Operations Manager or SOAU Covert Applications Team Manager, prior to seeking a warrant under the Act.

The applicant will be responsible for providing all evidence and intelligence to support the application for the warrant. The applicant will be required to swear or affirm the affidavit before a Justice of the Peace once completed and approved.

A SOAU Covert Applications Team member will attend The Family Court of Western Australia or Administrative Appeals Tribunal (AAT) on behalf of the applicant. The applicant should be contactable via telephone, should the Judge/AAT Member require any clarification, or ancillary information, or require the applicant to provide additional sworn evidence on the application.

The applicant must ensure appropriate records are made of the disclosure/communication and use of all ‘Protected Information’ obtained through the execution of a warrant under the Act.

If the applicant to whom a warrant is issued believes that the use of a surveillance device under the warrant is no longer necessary for the purpose of enabling evidence to be obtained of the commission of the relevant offence, or the identity or location of the offender, (or if in relation to a recovery order, for the purpose of enabling the location and safe recovery of the child to whom the order relates) the applicant MUST, pursuant to section 21(5) of the Act, apply for the warrant to be revoked.

The applicant is required to complete and submit a Destruction Advice Report to the OIC of SOAU with a recommendation as to whether the records and reports are to be destroyed or retained.

Where responsibility ceases to exist for the applicant for reasons such as extended leave or departure from the business unit, it is the responsibility of the applicant to nominate another person as the new law enforcement officer primarily responsible for executing the warrant or authorisation by contacting the SOAU Manager.

All Tasking Request Forms (TRF) submitted by an applicant for use of warrants under the Act must be approved by their relevant supervisor and OIC and be in accordance with an approved Investigation Strategy.

sections.h: Legal advice and guidance for applicants in respect to the obtaining of warrants and the exercising of powers under the Act are available via Legal Services Branch, or the State Solicitor’s Office as required.
sections.b: Legal advice and guidance for applicants in respect to the obtaining of warrants and the exercising of powers under the Act are available via Legal Services Branch, or the State Solicitor’s Office as required.

sections.h:
The SOAU Covert Application Supervisor is available to provide investigators or applicants guidance in the preparation of documentation under the Act.

Following an approved IDM TRF, SOAU Covert Application Team members (upon receipt of an Assessment Form) will prepare affidavits from the applicant and prepare the warrant and all other associated documentation required for presentation to the relevant Judge or AAT Member.

On a warrant being issued, a SOAU Covert Application Team member will provide information outlining the responsibilities of the applicant for the warrant in accordance with the Act.

SOAU will maintain security and storage of warrant documents in accordance with legislative requirements and advice provided by the oversight bodies (Commonwealth Ombudsman and Compliance Unit).

SOAU are responsible for the collation, preparation and presentation of Chief Officer Reports pursuant to sections 49(1) and 50(1) of the Act. The OIC of SOAU will ensure these reports are prepared and presented to the Chief Officer, or his authorised delegate in accordance with the legislative timeframes provided.

SOAU are responsible for the preparation and presentation of Warrant Revocations and Destruction Requests pursuant to sections 20(2) and 46(1)(b) of the Act to the Chief Officer (or delegate).

SOAU are responsible for maintaining a General Register pursuant to section 53 of the Act.

SOAU will maintain responsibility for the retention and destruction of all Protected Information, ensuring destruction is undertaken in accordance with section 46 of the Act.

SOAU will maintain responsibility for the retention and destruction of all Protected Information, ensuring destruction is undertaken in accordance with section 46 of the Act.

sections.h:
The OIC of SOAU maintains overall responsibility for ensuring WA Police Force policies and procedures regarding the Act are up to date and contemporary.

The OIC shall ensure advice/suggestions provided by the oversight bodies are considered and take all required action to implement changes to procedures and processes to ensure compliance.

The OIC may change processes and procedures as required if it is identified a current practice or procedure will result in non-compliance.

It is the responsibility of the OIC to ensure all legislative reporting obligations of the Chief Officer are undertaken within the allocated timeframes.

The OIC will maintain and ensure any delegation instrument pursuant to section 63 of the Act is contemporary and reflective of ranks and positions for which Chief Officer powers or functions have been delegated.

The OIC of SOAU will be required to facilitate inspections performed by the Commonwealth Ombudsman as required.

The OIC shall ensure advice/suggestions provided by the oversight bodies are considered and take all required action to implement changes to procedures and processes to ensure compliance. The OIC may change processes and procedures as required if it is identified a current practice or procedure will result in non-compliance. The OIC will maintain and ensure any delegation instrument pursuant to section 63 of the Act is contemporary and reflective of ranks and positions for which Chief Officer powers or functions have been delegated. The OIC of SOAU will be required to facilitate inspections performed by the Commonwealth Ombudsman as required.

sections.h:
The OIC PTU is responsible for the technical execution of warrants obtained pursuant to the Act.

It will be the responsibility of the OIC PTU to ensure all internal processes and guidelines are in accordance with the WA Police Force Procedures (this document) and the Act. Guidelines in regard to the collection, storage, security, and destruction of Protected Information are to be regularly reviewed to ensure best practice and agency compliance is maintained.

The OIC PTU will be required to participate in Ombudsman inspections as requested and will be responsible for the implementation of suggestions and guidance provided in respect to the technical deployment of resources pursuant to the Act.
sections.b: The OIC PTU is responsible for the technical execution of warrants obtained pursuant to the Act. It will be the responsibility of the OIC PTU to ensure all internal processes and guidelines are in accordance with the WA Police Force Procedures (this document) and the Act. Guidelines in regard to the collection, storage, security, and destruction of Protected Information are to be regularly reviewed to ensure best practice and agency compliance is maintained. The OIC PTU will be required to participate in Ombudsman inspections as requested and will be responsible for the implementation of suggestions and guidance provided in respect to the technical deployment of resources pursuant to the Act.

sections.h:
The WA Police Force Ethical Standards Division Compliance Unit will undertake independent inspections to ensure compliance with legislative and policy requirements are being maintained, pursuant to the Act and Ombudsman recommendations. Findings from the inspections will be reported to the business area’s Superintendent, Commander State Crime and the Commonwealth Ombudsman.

The Compliance Unit will work closely with the OIC SOAU, SOAU members and OIC PTU to ensure ongoing review of practices, processes and procedures, suggesting and implementing changes as required to maintain legislative compliance.
sections.b: The WA Police Force Ethical Standards Division Compliance Unit will undertake independent inspections to ensure compliance with legislative and policy requirements are being maintained, pursuant to the Act and Ombudsman recommendations. Findings from the inspections will be reported to the business area’s Superintendent, Commander State Crime and the Commonwealth Ombudsman. The Compliance Unit will work closely with the OIC SOAU, SOAU members and OIC PTU to ensure ongoing review of practices, processes and procedures, suggesting and implementing changes as required to maintain legislative compliance.

sections.h:
All applications for warrants, extensions or variations made under the Act will be prepared by SOAU on behalf of WA Police Force to ensure all record keeping, reporting and destruction is accurate and performed in accordance with legislation.

All applications for warrants pursuant to the Act will commence with the application of a TRF submitted via the Operations Coordinator.

Upon approval of a TRF the applicant will be provided guidance and assistance in the application and court processes.

All additional applications (extensions, variations etc.) will require submission of a supplementary TRF to the Operations Coordinator.
sections.b: All applications for warrants, extensions or variations made under the Act will be prepared by SOAU on behalf of WA Police Force to ensure all record keeping, reporting and destruction is accurate and performed in accordance with legislation. All applications for warrants pursuant to the Act will commence with the application of a TRF submitted via the Operations Coordinator. Upon approval of a TRF the applicant will be provided guidance and assistance in the application and court processes. All additional applications (extensions, variations etc.) will require submission of a supplementary TRF to the Operations Coordinator.

sections.t: Making Applications for Surveillance Device Warrants
sections.h:
SOAU Covert Application Team members will assist an Investigator/applicant to obtain a warrant pursuant to the Act.

All formal requests for warrants under the Act are to be commenced via the submission of a TRF. It is recommended the investigator/applicant communicate with the SOAU Operations Coordinator prior to submission of the TRF to ensure the request is appropriate.

The provisions of the Act allow the use of surveillance devices as a tool to assist in the investigation of a relevant offence. Applications for warrants, emergency authorisations and tracking device authorisations under the provisions of the Act must comply with the legislation. The provisions of the Act regulate:

there are different types of surveillance devices, including a data surveillance device, a listening device, an optical surveillance device and a tracking device;

types of warrant, including a surveillance device warrant, retrieval warrant, computer access warrant, data disruption warrant and network activity warrant; and three different locations whereby surveillance devices, subject to a warrant, can be installed, including in a specified premise, a specified object and with respect to a specified person.

An application for a surveillance device warrant can be made either in writing or orally (usually by telephone) in accordance with Section 14, ss6 and ss7 and Section 15 of the Act. The nature and circumstances that require the use of the surveillance device will determine how the application is made.
sections.b: SOAU Covert Application Team members will assist an Investigator/applicant to obtain a warrant pursuant to the Act. All formal requests for warrants under the Act are to be commenced via the submission of a TRF. It is recommended the investigator/applicant communicate with the SOAU Operations Coordinator prior to submission of the TRF to ensure the request is appropriate. The provisions of the Act allow the use of surveillance devices as a tool to assist in the investigation of a relevant offence. Applications for warrants, emergency authorisations and tracking device authorisations under the provisions of the Act must comply with the legislation. The provisions of the Act regulate: there are different types of surveillance devices, including a data surveillance device, a listening device, an optical surveillance device and a tracking device; types of warrant, including a surveillance device warrant, retrieval warrant, computer access warrant, data disruption warrant and network activity warrant; and three different locations whereby surveillance devices, subject to a warrant, can be installed, including in a specified premise, a specified object and with respect to a specified person. An application for a surveillance device warrant can be made either in writing or orally (usually by telephone) in accordance with Section 14, ss6 and ss7 and Section 15 of the Act. The nature and circumstances that require the use of the surveillance device will determine how the application is made.

sections.h:
Upon advice from the Operations Coordinator to proceed, the investigator/applicant will complete an approved Assessment Form, by electronic means to SOAU containing the facts and other grounds on which the application for the surveillance device warrant is based. The affidavit will be prepared by a SOAU Covert Application Team member.

Where possible, the affidavit will be sworn/affirmed by the Investigator/applicant in charge of the investigation, however another officer of the WA Police Force may make an application on that person’s behalf. SOAU will be responsible for making applications on behalf of members located in regional Western Australia.

The Covert Application Team Supervisor is responsible for checking the entire application to ensure that it complies with the legislation and that the documentation is of an appropriate standard to be submitted to an eligible Judge or nominated AAT member.

When complete and ready for signature, the investigating officer is to perform a final check of the application for accuracy and content and sign the affidavit before a Justice of the Peace.

SOAU will collate the documents required to proceed to Court, which includes:

the affidavit, sworn by the member in charge of the investigation;

the application cover sheet, appropriately endorsed with the relevant information and signed by the applicant;

the warrant form, appropriately endorsed with the relevant information; and an expiry date indicator.

PLEASE NOTE: Affidavits should include specific details about the privacy impacts on any person, and the existence of alternative means (and/or more traditional forms of surveillance) of obtaining evidence. When using particularly intrusive technologies, WA Police must provide sufficient detail in affidavits to enable the issuing authority to have regard to the use and capability of the technology, and the extent to which the privacy of any person is likely to be affected.
sections.b: Upon advice from the Operations Coordinator to proceed, the investigator/applicant will complete an approved Assessment Form, by electronic means to SOAU containing the facts and other grounds on which the application for the surveillance device warrant is based. The affidavit will be prepared by a SOAU Covert Application Team member. Where possible, the affidavit will be sworn/affirmed by the Investigator/applicant in charge of the investigation, however another officer of the WA Police Force may make an application on that person’s behalf. SOAU will be responsible for making applications on behalf of members located in regional Western Australia. The Covert Application Team Supervisor is responsible for checking the entire application to ensure that it complies with the legislation and that the documentation is of an appropriate standard to be submitted to an eligible Judge or nominated AAT member. When complete and ready for signature, the investigating officer is to perform a final check of the application for accuracy and content and sign the affidavit before a Justice of the Peace. SOAU will collate the documents required to proceed to Court, which includes: the affidavit, sworn by the member in charge of the investigation; the application cover sheet, appropriately endorsed with the relevant information and signed by the applicant; the warrant form, appropriately endorsed with the relevant information; and an expiry date indicator. PLEASE NOTE: Affidavits should include specific details about the privacy impacts on any person, and the existence of alternative means (and/or more traditional forms of surveillance) of obtaining evidence. When using particularly intrusive technologies, WA Police must provide sufficient detail in affidavits to enable the issuing authority to have regard to the use and capability of the technology, and the extent to which the privacy of any person is likely to be affected.

sections.t: Making Applications for Extensions/variations to Surveillance Device Warrants
sections.h:
An officer of WA Police Force may apply for an extension to the period a warrant is in force, up to a maximum of 90 days after the day the warrant would have otherwise expired, or for a variation of any of the other terms of the warrant.

Should an Investigator/applicant seek to extend or vary a warrant issued pursuant to the Act, an Investigator/applicant should first contact SOAU and a supplementary TRF is required to be submitted through the SOAU Operations Coordinator.

Following TRF approval and receipt of a completed Assessment Form containing the additional information, a SOAU Covert Application Team member will prepare the required affidavit/s and accompanying documents to obtain the required extension or variation from an eligible Judge or nominated AAT member. SOAU are responsible for making applications for extensions or variations on behalf of regional Western Australia.

As per the original application, the application to extend or vary the warrant will be reviewed by the SOAU Covert Application Team Supervisor. Upon review, the Investigator/applicant will check the application for accuracy and content and sign the affidavit before a Justice of the Peace.

SOAU will collate the documents required to proceed to court and will arrange for the warrant application to be heard by an eligible Judge or nominated AAT member as per the process outlined in the ATTENDING THE FAMILY COURT OF AUSTRALIA OR ADMINISTRATIVE APPEALS TRIBUNAL, section of this document.
sections.b: An officer of WA Police Force may apply for an extension to the period a warrant is in force, up to a maximum of 90 days after the day the warrant would have otherwise expired, or for a variation of any of the other terms of the warrant. Should an Investigator/applicant seek to extend or vary a warrant issued pursuant to the Act, an Investigator/applicant should first contact SOAU and a supplementary TRF is required to be submitted through the SOAU Operations Coordinator. Following TRF approval and receipt of a completed Assessment Form containing the additional information, a SOAU Covert Application Team member will prepare the required affidavit/s and accompanying documents to obtain the required extension or variation from an eligible Judge or nominated AAT member. SOAU are responsible for making applications for extensions or variations on behalf of regional Western Australia. As per the original application, the application to extend or vary the warrant will be reviewed by the SOAU Covert Application Team Supervisor. Upon review, the Investigator/applicant will check the application for accuracy and content and sign the affidavit before a Justice of the Peace. SOAU will collate the documents required to proceed to court and will arrange for the warrant application to be heard by an eligible Judge or nominated AAT member as per the process outlined in the ATTENDING THE FAMILY COURT OF AUSTRALIA OR ADMINISTRATIVE APPEALS TRIBUNAL, section of this document.

sections.h:
The Act provides for an officer of WA Police Force to apply for a retrieval warrant in respect of a surveillance device that was lawfully installed under a surveillance device warrant or a tracking device authorisation.

A supplementary TRF is required to be considered by the SOAU Operations Coordination Supervisor. The application must be made to an eligible Judge or a nominated AAT member and must be accompanied by an affidavit setting out the grounds on which the retrieval warrant is sought. A SOAU Covert Applications Team member will prepare the required affidavit/s and accompanying documents to obtain the required retrieval warrant from an eligible Judge or nominated AAT member.

The process for obtaining a Retrieval Warrant is the same as the process for obtaining the initial warrant with an affidavit prepared and signed, and reviewed by the SOAU Covert Application Team Supervisor.

SOAU will arrange for the application to be heard by an eligible Judge or nominated AAT member as per the process outlined at ATTENDING THE FAMILY COURT OF AUSTRALIA OR ADMINISTRATIVE APPEALS TRIBUNAL.
sections.b: The Act provides for an officer of WA Police Force to apply for a retrieval warrant in respect of a surveillance device that was lawfully installed under a surveillance device warrant or a tracking device authorisation. A supplementary TRF is required to be considered by the SOAU Operations Coordination Supervisor. The application must be made to an eligible Judge or a nominated AAT member and must be accompanied by an affidavit setting out the grounds on which the retrieval warrant is sought. A SOAU Covert Applications Team member will prepare the required affidavit/s and accompanying documents to obtain the required retrieval warrant from an eligible Judge or nominated AAT member. The process for obtaining a Retrieval Warrant is the same as the process for obtaining the initial warrant with an affidavit prepared and signed, and reviewed by the SOAU Covert Application Team Supervisor. SOAU will arrange for the application to be heard by an eligible Judge or nominated AAT member as per the process outlined at ATTENDING THE FAMILY COURT OF AUSTRALIA OR ADMINISTRATIVE APPEALS TRIBUNAL.

sections.t: Attending the Family Court of Australia or Administrative Appeals Tribunal
sections.h:
SOAU staff will contact the court officer at the Family Court of Australia or the AAT and advise the court officer that WA Police Force wish to proceed with the matter and request that an eligible Judge or nominated AAT member be made available to consider the warrant.

A SOAU Covert Applications Team member will attend the Family Court of Australia or the AAT with the relevant documentation on behalf of the Investigator/applicant. The Investigator/applicant will be available via telephone at the request of the eligible Judge or nominated AAT member to clarify or provide further sworn evidence if required.
sections.b: SOAU staff will contact the court officer at the Family Court of Australia or the AAT and advise the court officer that WA Police Force wish to proceed with the matter and request that an eligible Judge or nominated AAT member be made available to consider the warrant. A SOAU Covert Applications Team member will attend the Family Court of Australia or the AAT with the relevant documentation on behalf of the Investigator/applicant. The Investigator/applicant will be available via telephone at the request of the eligible Judge or nominated AAT member to clarify or provide further sworn evidence if required.

sections.h:
Once a warrant is issued, the surveillance device warrant and all documentation is to be conveyed by a SOAU member to the SOAU Warrant Administration Team for the purposes of recording and compliance. SOAU will provide a copy of the warrant to the Investigator/applicant and PTU.

On the warrant being issued, the SOAU Covert Applications Team member will provide the Investigator/applicant information via email pertaining to their responsibilities during the period of the warrant.

Should the eligible Judge or nominated AAT member refuse to grant a warrant, this information must be reported on the General Register, along with the reason for the refusal. The OIC of SOAU is to be advised, who in turn will liaise with the investigating officer and determine whether or not to make an amended application in relation to the warrant sought. All application documents must be kept and retained by SOAU.
sections.b: Once a warrant is issued, the surveillance device warrant and all documentation is to be conveyed by a SOAU member to the SOAU Warrant Administration Team for the purposes of recording and compliance. SOAU will provide a copy of the warrant to the Investigator/applicant and PTU. On the warrant being issued, the SOAU Covert Applications Team member will provide the Investigator/applicant information via email pertaining to their responsibilities during the period of the warrant. Should the eligible Judge or nominated AAT member refuse to grant a warrant, this information must be reported on the General Register, along with the reason for the refusal. The OIC of SOAU is to be advised, who in turn will liaise with the investigating officer and determine whether or not to make an amended application in relation to the warrant sought. All application documents must be kept and retained by SOAU.

sections.h:
Under certain urgent circumstances, an appropriate authorising officer may consider applications for an emergency authorisation for the use of a surveillance device which would otherwise require a warrant. Before making an application to an appropriate authorising officer for an emergency authorisation, the applicant must reasonably suspect that:

in relation to an investigation of a relevant offence:

an imminent risk of serious violence to a person or substantial damage to property exists;

the use of a surveillance device is immediately necessary for the purpose of dealing with that risk;

the circumstances are so serious and the matter is of such urgency that the use of a surveillance device is warranted; and it is not practicable in the circumstances to apply for a surveillance device warrant; or in relation to a recovery order that is in force:

the circumstances are so urgent as to warrant the immediate use of a surveillance device; and it is not practicable in the circumstances to apply for a surveillance device warrant.
sections.b: Under certain urgent circumstances, an appropriate authorising officer may consider applications for an emergency authorisation for the use of a surveillance device which would otherwise require a warrant. Before making an application to an appropriate authorising officer for an emergency authorisation, the applicant must reasonably suspect that: in relation to an investigation of a relevant offence: an imminent risk of serious violence to a person or substantial damage to property exists; the use of a surveillance device is immediately necessary for the purpose of dealing with that risk; the circumstances are so serious and the matter is of such urgency that the use of a surveillance device is warranted; and it is not practicable in the circumstances to apply for a surveillance device warrant; or in relation to a recovery order that is in force: the circumstances are so urgent as to warrant the immediate use of a surveillance device; and it is not practicable in the circumstances to apply for a surveillance device warrant.

sections.h:
Section 6 of the Act defines an Appropriate Authorising officer as:

(a) of a law enforcement agency—has the meaning given by subsection 6A(4); or

(b) in relation to a law enforcement officer belonging to or seconded to a law enforcement agency—means an appropriate authorising officer of the law enforcement agency.

Section 6A(4) provides a table defining the ranks or classifications of an Appropriate Authorising Officer, which for WA Police Force are:

the Chief Officer or the person holding equivalent rank; or an Assistant Commissioner (State Crime) or a person acting in the position; or a Commander (State Crime) or a person acting in the position.

WA Police Force will seek emergency authorisations from a person holding the rank of (or equivalent rank) an Assistant Commissioner (State Crime).

Upon application for an Emergency Authorisation SOAU will coordinate, obtain access to and assist the applicant and relevant Authorising Officer.
sections.b: Section 6 of the Act defines an Appropriate Authorising officer as: (a) of a law enforcement agency—has the meaning given by subsection 6A(4); or (b) in relation to a law enforcement officer belonging to or seconded to a law enforcement agency—means an appropriate authorising officer of the law enforcement agency. Section 6A(4) provides a table defining the ranks or classifications of an Appropriate Authorising Officer, which for WA Police Force are: the Chief Officer or the person holding equivalent rank; or an Assistant Commissioner (State Crime) or a person acting in the position; or a Commander (State Crime) or a person acting in the position. WA Police Force will seek emergency authorisations from a person holding the rank of (or equivalent rank) an Assistant Commissioner (State Crime). Upon application for an Emergency Authorisation SOAU will coordinate, obtain access to and assist the applicant and relevant Authorising Officer.

sections.h:
An application for an Emergency Authorisation may be made orally, in writing or by telephone, fax, email or any other means of communication. The appropriate authorising officer may give the emergency authorisation if satisfied that there are reasonable grounds for the suspicion founding the application.

Should an appropriate authorising officer give an emergency authorisation, that officer must, as soon as practicable, make a written record of the giving of that authorisation, including in the record:

the name of the applicant for the authorisation;

the date and time the authorisation was given; and

the nature of the authorisation given.

This original written record made by the appropriate authorising officer is to be provided to the OIC of SOAU, who will cause the record to be filed with the appropriate documentation and ensure the details of the emergency authorisation are entered into the General Register.

Within 48 hours after giving an emergency authorisation the appropriate authorising officer who gave the authorisation, or another person on the appropriate authorising officer’s behalf, must apply to an eligible Judge or nominated AAT member for approval of the giving of the emergency authorisation.

It is possible during the same application to apply for a warrant authorising the ongoing use of the surveillance device, providing all the normal legislative requirements are met as per a standard warrant application under the Act. The application:

must specify:

the name of the applicant for the approval; and

the kind or kinds of surveillance device to which the emergency authorisation relates and, if a warrant is sought, the nature and duration of the warrant;

must be supported by an affidavit setting out the grounds on which the approval (and warrant, if any) is sought; and must be accompanied by a copy of the written record made after the giving of the emergency authorisation.

A copy of this application must be kept in the relevant Codafile by the responsible SOAU officer.

SOAU will assist the appropriate authorising officer, or the officer acting on the appropriate authorising officer’s behalf, in relation to this application, in a manner substantially similar to the process followed in the normal application for a surveillance device warrant outlined by the Form of Application.
