sections.t: Making Applications for Tracking Device Authorisations and Retrieval of Tracking Devices
sections.h:
Under certain circumstances, an appropriate authorising officer may give a tracking device authorisation to an officer of WA Police Force, authorising the use of a tracking device without a warrant in the investigation of a relevant offence, or in the location and safe recovery of a child to whom a recovery order relates.

The appropriate authorising officer may also authorise the retrieval, without a warrant, of a tracking device to which a tracking device authorisation relates.

An appropriate authorising officer must not give permission under the Act for the use, installation or retrieval of a tracking device if the installation of the device, or its retrieval, involves entry onto premises without permission or an interference with the interior of a vehicle without permission.

The applicant, being an officer of WA Police Force, must apply orally or in writing to the appropriate authorising officer and must address in that application the matters that would be required to be addressed as if the officer were making an application for a surveillance device warrant or a retrieval warrant, as the case requires.

Should an appropriate authorising officer give a tracking device authorisation, that officer must, as soon as practicable, make a written record of the giving of that authorisation, including in the record:

the name of the applicant for the authorisation;

the date and time the authorisation was given;

the nature of the authorisation given, including where applicable:

the alleged offence or offences in respect of which the authorisation is given;

if in relation to a recovery order, the date the order was made and the name of the child to whom the order relates;

the object or class of object in or on which the use of the tracking device is authorised;

the vehicle or class of vehicle on which the use of the tracking device is authorised;

the name of the specified person of which the use of the tracking device is authorised; and the premises or object from which the tracking device is to be retrieved, if authorising the retrieval of a tracking device;

the name of the officer of WA Police Force who is primarily responsible for executing the authorisation; and any conditions subject to which a tracking device may be used, under the authorisation.

This original written record made by the appropriate authorising officer is to be provided to the OIC of SOAU, who will cause the record to be filed with the appropriate documentation and ensure the details of the tracking device authorisation are entered into the General Register.
sections.b: Under certain circumstances, an appropriate authorising officer may give a tracking device authorisation to an officer of WA Police Force, authorising the use of a tracking device without a warrant in the investigation of a relevant offence, or in the location and safe recovery of a child to whom a recovery order relates. The appropriate authorising officer may also authorise the retrieval, without a warrant, of a tracking device to which a tracking device authorisation relates. An appropriate authorising officer must not give permission under the Act for the use, installation or retrieval of a tracking device if the installation of the device, or its retrieval, involves entry onto premises without permission or an interference with the interior of a vehicle without permission. The applicant, being an officer of WA Police Force, must apply orally or in writing to the appropriate authorising officer and must address in that application the matters that would be required to be addressed as if the officer were making an application for a surveillance device warrant or a retrieval warrant, as the case requires. Should an appropriate authorising officer give a tracking device authorisation, that officer must, as soon as practicable, make a written record of the giving of that authorisation, including in the record: the name of the applicant for the authorisation; the date and time the authorisation was given; the nature of the authorisation given, including where applicable: the alleged offence or offences in respect of which the authorisation is given; if in relation to a recovery order, the date the order was made and the name of the child to whom the order relates; the object or class of object in or on which the use of the tracking device is authorised; the vehicle or class of vehicle on which the use of the tracking device is authorised; the name of the specified person of which the use of the tracking device is authorised; and the premises or object from which the tracking device is to be retrieved, if authorising the retrieval of a tracking device; the name of the officer of WA Police Force who is primarily responsible for executing the authorisation; and any conditions subject to which a tracking device may be used, under the authorisation. This original written record made by the appropriate authorising officer is to be provided to the OIC of SOAU, who will cause the record to be filed with the appropriate documentation and ensure the details of the tracking device authorisation are entered into the General Register.

sections.h:
Computer access warrants, which allows law enforcement:

to covertly access devices to investigate serious crimes;

to search devices such as laptops, mobile phones, USBs, and collect information; and to conceal the fact that a device has been accessed.

Law enforcement computer access warrants must be issued by an independent authority (a Judge or AAT member) and cannot authorise interference with, or material loss or damage to, a computer.

Computer access warrants can be sought only for serious offences (offences that attract a penalty of three years or more).

Division 4, section 27A of the Act (Computer Access Warrants) provides for the use of Computer Access Warrants.
sections.b: Computer access warrants, which allows law enforcement: to covertly access devices to investigate serious crimes; to search devices such as laptops, mobile phones, USBs, and collect information; and to conceal the fact that a device has been accessed. Law enforcement computer access warrants must be issued by an independent authority (a Judge or AAT member) and cannot authorise interference with, or material loss or damage to, a computer. Computer access warrants can be sought only for serious offences (offences that attract a penalty of three years or more). Division 4, section 27A of the Act (Computer Access Warrants) provides for the use of Computer Access Warrants.

sections.t: Making Applications for A Computer Access Warrant
sections.h:
An Investigator/applicant seeking to obtain a Computer Access Warrant shall contact SOAU to discuss the application prior to submission of a formal TRF.

An Investigator/applicant will complete an Assessment Form and accompanying documentation and submit it to the SOAU Covert Applications Team for preparation of an affidavit to obtain a Computer Access Warrant.

Procedures for the obtaining the Computer Access Warrant via an eligible a Judge or AAT member and Investigator/applicant responsibilities remain consistent with the same processes of obtaining a Surveillance Device Warrant.
sections.b: An Investigator/applicant seeking to obtain a Computer Access Warrant shall contact SOAU to discuss the application prior to submission of a formal TRF. An Investigator/applicant will complete an Assessment Form and accompanying documentation and submit it to the SOAU Covert Applications Team for preparation of an affidavit to obtain a Computer Access Warrant. Procedures for the obtaining the Computer Access Warrant via an eligible a Judge or AAT member and Investigator/applicant responsibilities remain consistent with the same processes of obtaining a Surveillance Device Warrant.

sections.h:
Data Surveillance is only possible under the provisions of the Act with deployment of any data surveillance devices undertaken by the PTU and with the existence of a data surveillance warrant.

Applications for a Surveillance Devices Act Warrant are made under section 14 of the Act, for a surveillance device warrant to be issued under section 16 of the Act.

Section 18(1)(c) of the Act provides for use of a surveillance device in respect of the conversations, activities or location of a specified person or person whose identity is unknown.

Each deployment of a data surveillance device will be undertaken in accordance with the Act in respect to collection, storage, communication, use and destruction of Protected Information obtained through the execution of the warrant.

In all instances a data surveillance device warrant is executed under the provisions of the Act, the following procedures must be adhered to and complied with.
sections.b: Data Surveillance is only possible under the provisions of the Act with deployment of any data surveillance devices undertaken by the PTU and with the existence of a data surveillance warrant. Applications for a Surveillance Devices Act Warrant are made under section 14 of the Act, for a surveillance device warrant to be issued under section 16 of the Act. Section 18(1)(c) of the Act provides for use of a surveillance device in respect of the conversations, activities or location of a specified person or person whose identity is unknown. Each deployment of a data surveillance device will be undertaken in accordance with the Act in respect to collection, storage, communication, use and destruction of Protected Information obtained through the execution of the warrant. In all instances a data surveillance device warrant is executed under the provisions of the Act, the following procedures must be adhered to and complied with.

sections.h:
WA Police PTU will utilise only an approved device for the collection of data under the provisions of a warrant issued pursuant to the Act.

An approved device is defined as a device authorised for use by the Commander, State Crime.

The device may be provided by another law enforcement or government agency whereby an appropriate Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) between the WA Police Force and other agency exists.

PTU staff with the required training and qualifications operate data surveillance devices.
sections.b: WA Police PTU will utilise only an approved device for the collection of data under the provisions of a warrant issued pursuant to the Act. An approved device is defined as a device authorised for use by the Commander, State Crime. The device may be provided by another law enforcement or government agency whereby an appropriate Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) between the WA Police Force and other agency exists. PTU staff with the required training and qualifications operate data surveillance devices.

sections.t: Storage of Data Obtained During A Data Surveillance Device Warrant:
sections.h:
All data obtained through the execution of a data surveillance device warrant issued pursuant to the Act, will be uploaded and stored upon the WA Police Force Digital Evidence Management System (PDEMS) with access to this data restricted only to persons requiring authorised access.

Protected information obtained through execution of a warrant and provided to an investigator will be provided to the investigator via the Intelligence Data Management (IDM) platform.
sections.b: All data obtained through the execution of a data surveillance device warrant issued pursuant to the Act, will be uploaded and stored upon the WA Police Force Digital Evidence Management System (PDEMS) with access to this data restricted only to persons requiring authorised access. Protected information obtained through execution of a warrant and provided to an investigator will be provided to the investigator via the Intelligence Data Management (IDM) platform.

sections.h:
As the collection of data through use of a data surveillance device will likely result in a large volume of data NOT relevant to the investigation, PTU will, upon upload to the PDEMS platform, review and undertake a vetting process to identify only the data which is relevant to the investigation for which the warrant was obtained.

Once the relevant information has been identified it is to be provided to the investigator via the IDM platform.

The investigator will be provided the relevant IDM reference and communication of the Protected Information is to be recorded within the relevant Use and Communication Register and PTU running sheets.

The remaining Protected Information will be secured within the PDEMS platform with access restricted as required until destroyed in accordance to a destruction regime pursuant to section 46(1)(b) of the Act (please refer to DESTRUCTION AND RETENTION OF PROTECTED INFORMATION).
sections.b: As the collection of data through use of a data surveillance device will likely result in a large volume of data NOT relevant to the investigation, PTU will, upon upload to the PDEMS platform, review and undertake a vetting process to identify only the data which is relevant to the investigation for which the warrant was obtained. Once the relevant information has been identified it is to be provided to the investigator via the IDM platform. The investigator will be provided the relevant IDM reference and communication of the Protected Information is to be recorded within the relevant Use and Communication Register and PTU running sheets. The remaining Protected Information will be secured within the PDEMS platform with access restricted as required until destroyed in accordance to a destruction regime pursuant to section 46(1)(b) of the Act (please refer to DESTRUCTION AND RETENTION OF PROTECTED INFORMATION).

sections.h:
Destruction of data obtained through the execution of data surveillance device warrant will be undertaken in accordance to a destruction regime pursuant to section 46(1)(b) of the Act.

Only upon issue of a Destruction Order is Protected Information stored within PDEMS and IDM to be destroyed.

Inquiries regarding the destruction of Protected Information should be directed to SOAU.

Any Protected Information obtained through execution of a data surveillance warrant held by another law enforcement or government agency shall be subject to the WA Police Force Destruction Process with evidence of the destruction required to be recorded within the warrant file.

SOAU will manage and coordinate the destruction of all protected information on behalf of the WA Police Force.
sections.b: Destruction of data obtained through the execution of data surveillance device warrant will be undertaken in accordance to a destruction regime pursuant to section 46(1)(b) of the Act. Only upon issue of a Destruction Order is Protected Information stored within PDEMS and IDM to be destroyed. Inquiries regarding the destruction of Protected Information should be directed to SOAU. Any Protected Information obtained through execution of a data surveillance warrant held by another law enforcement or government agency shall be subject to the WA Police Force Destruction Process with evidence of the destruction required to be recorded within the warrant file. SOAU will manage and coordinate the destruction of all protected information on behalf of the WA Police Force.

sections.t: Making Applications for A Data Disruption or Network Activity Warrant
sections.h:
An Investigator/applicant seeking to obtain a Data Disruption or Network Activity Warrant shall contact SOAU to discuss the application prior to submission of a formal TRF.

An Investigator/applicant will complete an Assessment Form and accompanying documentation and submit it to the SOAU Covert Applications Team for preparation of an affidavit to obtain a Data Disruption or Network Activity Warrant.

Procedures for the obtaining the Data Disruption or Network Activity Warrant via an eligible a Judge or AAT member and Investigator/applicant responsibilities remain consistent with the same processes of obtaining a Surveillance Device Warrant.
sections.b: An Investigator/applicant seeking to obtain a Data Disruption or Network Activity Warrant shall contact SOAU to discuss the application prior to submission of a formal TRF. An Investigator/applicant will complete an Assessment Form and accompanying documentation and submit it to the SOAU Covert Applications Team for preparation of an affidavit to obtain a Data Disruption or Network Activity Warrant. Procedures for the obtaining the Data Disruption or Network Activity Warrant via an eligible a Judge or AAT member and Investigator/applicant responsibilities remain consistent with the same processes of obtaining a Surveillance Device Warrant.

sections.h:
There are a number of situations that may occur where a surveillance device warrant may, or must, be revoked.

A surveillance device warrant may, by instrument in writing, be revoked by an eligible Judge or nominated AAT member on his or her own initiative at any time before the expiration of the period of validity specified in the warrant. The eligible Judge or nominated AAT member must give a copy of the instrument of revocation to the Chief Officer, who in turn will forward the copy of the instrument of revocation to the OIC SOAU, who will cause the copy of the instrument of revocation to be filed in the relevant Codafile and ensure the necessary updates in the General Register are made.

Pursuant to section 21(5) of the Act, if the investigating officer to whom a warrant is issued believes that the use of a surveillance device under the warrant is no longer necessary for the purpose of enabling evidence to be obtained of the commission of the relevant offence, or the identity or location of the offender, (or if in relation to a recovery order, for the purpose of enabling the location and safe recovery of the child to whom the order relates) the investigating officer must immediately inform the Chief Officer (via the OIC SOAU) of that belief.

If the Chief Officer is satisfied that the use of a surveillance device under the warrant sought is no longer necessary for the purpose of enabling evidence to be obtained of the commission of the relevant offence, or the identity or location of the offender, (or if in relation to a recovery order, for the purpose of enabling the location and safe recovery of the child to whom the order relates) the Chief Officer must:

by instrument in writing, revoke the warrant; and

take the steps necessary to ensure that use of the surveillance device authorised by the warrant is discontinued.

This instrument of revocation is to be forwarded to the OIC of SOAU who will cause the instrument of revocation to be filed in the relevant Codafile and ensure the necessary updates in the General Register are made.

If the Chief Officer is satisfied that the use of a surveillance device under the warrant sought is no longer necessary for the purpose of enabling evidence to be obtained of the commission of the relevant offence, or the identity or location of the offender, (or if in relation to a recovery order, for the purpose of enabling the location and safe recovery of the child to whom the order relates) the Chief Officer must: by instrument in writing, revoke the warrant; and take the steps necessary to ensure that use of the surveillance device authorised by the warrant is discontinued. This instrument of revocation is to be forwarded to the OIC of SOAU who will cause the instrument of revocation to be filed in the relevant Codafile and ensure the necessary updates in the General Register are made.

sections.h:
On issue of a Commonwealth SDA warrant, investigators will be advised about revocation requirements. This includes the requirement for law enforcement officers to notify the Chief Officer immediately upon forming the belief that use of a surveillance device under the warrant is no longer necessary for its original purpose.

SOAU will provide a prompt to investigators each month after the issue of a Commonwealth SDA warrant, to ascertain if the warrant is still required.

If the warrant is no longer required SOAU will seek a revocation in writing from the Chief Officer as soon as practicable on notification that the warrant is no longer required and in any event within two weeks of that notification.

Records of the correspondence, including the prompts will be kept on file.
sections.b: On issue of a Commonwealth SDA warrant, investigators will be advised about revocation requirements. This includes the requirement for law enforcement officers to notify the Chief Officer immediately upon forming the belief that use of a surveillance device under the warrant is no longer necessary for its original purpose. SOAU will provide a prompt to investigators each month after the issue of a Commonwealth SDA warrant, to ascertain if the warrant is still required. If the warrant is no longer required SOAU will seek a revocation in writing from the Chief Officer as soon as practicable on notification that the warrant is no longer required and in any event within two weeks of that notification. Records of the correspondence, including the prompts will be kept on file.

sections.h:
Upon receipt of an approved TRF for a warrant, an emergency authorisation or a tracking device authorisation, the responsible SOAU officer will cause any documentation to be saved electronically on the SOAU secure network drive in the appropriate folder. Any further documentation concerning the warrant, emergency authorisation or tracking device authorisation that is created during the application process, including supporting affidavits, is also to be saved in the appropriate folder.

Requests for access to the SOAU secure network drive will be considered by the OIC of SOAU.
sections.b: Upon receipt of an approved TRF for a warrant, an emergency authorisation or a tracking device authorisation, the responsible SOAU officer will cause any documentation to be saved electronically on the SOAU secure network drive in the appropriate folder. Any further documentation concerning the warrant, emergency authorisation or tracking device authorisation that is created during the application process, including supporting affidavits, is also to be saved in the appropriate folder. Requests for access to the SOAU secure network drive will be considered by the OIC of SOAU.

sections.h:
All hard copy applications, affidavits, warrants, emergency authorisations, tracking device authorisations and associated documentation will be stored at SOAU. Once the warrant, emergency authorisation or tracking device authorisation application is approved by the eligible Judge or nominated AAT member, or authorising officer, the SOAU officer will convey the hard copy applications, affidavit, signed warrant, emergency authorisation or tracking device authorisation and associated documentation to the Warrant Administration section of SOAU for storage, reporting and record keeping.

All hard copy documents will be stored in a restricted-access secure area at SOAU in a Codafile specific to that warrant, emergency authorisation or tracking device authorisation. Requests for access to SOAU restricted-access secure area will be considered by the OIC of SOAU.
sections.b: All hard copy applications, affidavits, warrants, emergency authorisations, tracking device authorisations and associated documentation will be stored at SOAU. Once the warrant, emergency authorisation or tracking device authorisation application is approved by the eligible Judge or nominated AAT member, or authorising officer, the SOAU officer will convey the hard copy applications, affidavit, signed warrant, emergency authorisation or tracking device authorisation and associated documentation to the Warrant Administration section of SOAU for storage, reporting and record keeping. All hard copy documents will be stored in a restricted-access secure area at SOAU in a Codafile specific to that warrant, emergency authorisation or tracking device authorisation. Requests for access to SOAU restricted-access secure area will be considered by the OIC of SOAU.

sections.h:
The responsible SOAU officer is to cause a Codafile to be created for each warrant, emergency authorisation and tracking device authorisation that is applied for by WA Police Force. Codafile creation materials are to be located in the stationery area of SOAU and can be re-ordered through the administration officer of SOAU.

The Codafile is to be appropriately labelled with the designator of the warrant, emergency authorisation or tracking device authorisation and will include a print-out of the cover sheet document on red card immediately inside.

Any person updating documentation within the Codafile is to ensure the checklist component of the cover sheet document is updated accordingly.
sections.b: The responsible SOAU officer is to cause a Codafile to be created for each warrant, emergency authorisation and tracking device authorisation that is applied for by WA Police Force. Codafile creation materials are to be located in the stationery area of SOAU and can be re-ordered through the administration officer of SOAU. The Codafile is to be appropriately labelled with the designator of the warrant, emergency authorisation or tracking device authorisation and will include a print-out of the cover sheet document on red card immediately inside. Any person updating documentation within the Codafile is to ensure the checklist component of the cover sheet document is updated accordingly.

sections.t: Documents Required to Be Kept in Codafile
sections.h:
SOAU will ensure the following documents are kept in the relevant Codafile for each warrant, emergency authorisation and tracking device authorisation applied for by WA Police Force under the Act:

Warrants:

each warrant issued;

each instrument of revocation, if applicable;

a copy of each application made for a warrant, or for an extension or variation of a warrant;

a copy of the completed Investigator Report document;

a copy of any relevant Communication of Protected Information forms;

a copy of any relevant Destruction Advice Reports; and

a copy of any evidentiary certificates issued pursuant to section 62 of the Act.

Emergency authorisations:

each written record made by the authorising officer after giving an emergency authorisation, pursuant to section 31 of the Act;

each written application for an emergency authorisation;

a copy of each application made pursuant to section 33 of the Act, in relation to approval of an emergency authorisation issued by an authorising officer;

a copy of the completed Investigator Report document;

a copy of any relevant Communication of Protected Information forms;

a copy of any relevant Destruction Advice Reports; and

a copy of any evidentiary certificates issued pursuant to section 62 of the Act.

Tracking device authorisations:

each written record made by the authorising officer after giving a tracking device authorisation, pursuant to section 40 of the Act;

each written application for a tracking device authorisation;

a copy of the completed Investigator Report document;

a copy of any relevant Communication of Protected Information forms;

a copy of any relevant Destruction Advice Reports; and

a copy of any evidentiary certificates issued pursuant to section 62 of the Act.
sections.b: SOAU will ensure the following documents are kept in the relevant Codafile for each warrant, emergency authorisation and tracking device authorisation applied for by WA Police Force under the Act: Warrants: each warrant issued; each instrument of revocation, if applicable; a copy of each application made for a warrant, or for an extension or variation of a warrant; a copy of the completed Investigator Report document; a copy of any relevant Communication of Protected Information forms; a copy of any relevant Destruction Advice Reports; and a copy of any evidentiary certificates issued pursuant to section 62 of the Act. Emergency authorisations: each written record made by the authorising officer after giving an emergency authorisation, pursuant to section 31 of the Act; each written application for an emergency authorisation; a copy of each application made pursuant to section 33 of the Act, in relation to approval of an emergency authorisation issued by an authorising officer; a copy of the completed Investigator Report document; a copy of any relevant Communication of Protected Information forms; a copy of any relevant Destruction Advice Reports; and a copy of any evidentiary certificates issued pursuant to section 62 of the Act. Tracking device authorisations: each written record made by the authorising officer after giving a tracking device authorisation, pursuant to section 40 of the Act; each written application for a tracking device authorisation; a copy of the completed Investigator Report document; a copy of any relevant Communication of Protected Information forms; a copy of any relevant Destruction Advice Reports; and a copy of any evidentiary certificates issued pursuant to section 62 of the Act.

sections.t: Other Documents Required to Be Kept
sections.h:
The responsible SOAU officer is to cause the following documents to be kept in an appropriate location:

a copy of each report made to the Minister pursuant to section 49 of the Act;

a statement as to whether each application made for a warrant, or for the extension or variation of a warrant, was granted, refused or withdrawn;

a statement as to whether each application made by an officer of Western Australia Police Force for an emergency authorisation was granted, refused or withdrawn;

a statement as to whether each application made by an appropriate authorising officer for approval of the giving of an emergency authorisation to an officer of Western Australia Police Force was granted, refused or withdrawn;

a statement as to whether each application made for a tracking device authorisation was granted, refused or withdrawn; and details of the destruction of records or reports, pursuant to section 46(1)(b) of the Act. See DESTRUCTION AND RETENTION OF PROTECTED INFORMATION.
sections.b: The responsible SOAU officer is to cause the following documents to be kept in an appropriate location: a copy of each report made to the Minister pursuant to section 49 of the Act; a statement as to whether each application made for a warrant, or for the extension or variation of a warrant, was granted, refused or withdrawn; a statement as to whether each application made by an officer of Western Australia Police Force for an emergency authorisation was granted, refused or withdrawn; a statement as to whether each application made by an appropriate authorising officer for approval of the giving of an emergency authorisation to an officer of Western Australia Police Force was granted, refused or withdrawn; a statement as to whether each application made for a tracking device authorisation was granted, refused or withdrawn; and details of the destruction of records or reports, pursuant to section 46(1)(b) of the Act. See DESTRUCTION AND RETENTION OF PROTECTED INFORMATION.

sections.h: The responsible SOAU officer is to maintain a General Register of warrants, emergency authorisations and tracking device authorisations applied for by WA Police Force, pursuant to section 53 of the Act.
sections.b: The responsible SOAU officer is to maintain a General Register of warrants, emergency authorisations and tracking device authorisations applied for by WA Police Force, pursuant to section 53 of the Act.

sections.h:
Upon a warrant, emergency authorisation or tracking device authorisation ceasing to be in force, either through expiration or revocation, the responsible SOAU officer is to forward the investigating officer a copy of the Investigator Report document. This report will form the basis for the Chief Officer’s report to the Minister, pursuant to section 49 of the Act.

The investigator is to complete this document as soon as practicable by entering the required details and return it via email to SOAU. The report is to be filed by the responsible SOAU officer in the relevant Codafile for the warrant, emergency authorisation or tracking device authorisation.
sections.b: Upon a warrant, emergency authorisation or tracking device authorisation ceasing to be in force, either through expiration or revocation, the responsible SOAU officer is to forward the investigating officer a copy of the Investigator Report document. This report will form the basis for the Chief Officer’s report to the Minister, pursuant to section 49 of the Act. The investigator is to complete this document as soon as practicable by entering the required details and return it via email to SOAU. The report is to be filed by the responsible SOAU officer in the relevant Codafile for the warrant, emergency authorisation or tracking device authorisation.

sections.h:
Upon a warrant, emergency authorisation or tracking device authorisation ceasing to be in force, either through expiration or revocation, the Chief Officer is required to, as soon as practicable, make a report to the Minister, pursuant to section 49 of the Act.

Utilising the completed Investigator Report, SOAU will prepare the relevant documentation in order for the Chief Officer to make his or her report. This will be forwarded to Chief Officer’s authorised delegate through the OIC of SOAU.

The prepared documentation is to include:

a draft cover letter for consideration by the Chief Officer authorised delegate;

a copy of the relevant warrants, emergency authorisations or tracking device authorisations;

a copy of any instrument revoking, extending or varying the relevant warrants, emergency authorisations or tracking device authorisations; and a copy of the completed Investigator Report document for the relevant warrants, emergency authorisations or tracking device authorisations.

SOAU is required to keep a copy of this report. The office of the Chief Officer will arrange for a final copy of the report sent from the Chief Officer to the Minister to be provided to SOAU for this purpose.
sections.b: Upon a warrant, emergency authorisation or tracking device authorisation ceasing to be in force, either through expiration or revocation, the Chief Officer is required to, as soon as practicable, make a report to the Minister, pursuant to section 49 of the Act. Utilising the completed Investigator Report, SOAU will prepare the relevant documentation in order for the Chief Officer to make his or her report. This will be forwarded to Chief Officer’s authorised delegate through the OIC of SOAU. The prepared documentation is to include: a draft cover letter for consideration by the Chief Officer authorised delegate; a copy of the relevant warrants, emergency authorisations or tracking device authorisations; a copy of any instrument revoking, extending or varying the relevant warrants, emergency authorisations or tracking device authorisations; and a copy of the completed Investigator Report document for the relevant warrants, emergency authorisations or tracking device authorisations. SOAU is required to keep a copy of this report. The office of the Chief Officer will arrange for a final copy of the report sent from the Chief Officer to the Minister to be provided to SOAU for this purpose.

sections.h:
The Chief Officer is required to, as soon as practicable after the end of each financial year, and in any event within 3 months after the end of the financial year, submit a report to the Minister in respect of the use of the Act by WA Police Force during that financial year.

On behalf of the Chief Officer, SOAU will prepare the relevant documentation required and forward to the office of the Chief Officer through the OIC of SOAU.

The information is to be presented in such a way as to identify the number of warrants issued, emergency authorisations given and tracking device authorisations given in respect of each different kind of surveillance device.
sections.b: The Chief Officer is required to, as soon as practicable after the end of each financial year, and in any event within 3 months after the end of the financial year, submit a report to the Minister in respect of the use of the Act by WA Police Force during that financial year. On behalf of the Chief Officer, SOAU will prepare the relevant documentation required and forward to the office of the Chief Officer through the OIC of SOAU. The information is to be presented in such a way as to identify the number of warrants issued, emergency authorisations given and tracking device authorisations given in respect of each different kind of surveillance device.

sections.h:
Protected information is defined as:

any information obtained from the use of a surveillance device under a warrant, an emergency authorisation or a tracking device authorisation, any information relating to an application for, the issue of, the existence of, or the expiration of, a warrant, an emergency authorisation or a tracking device authorisation;

any information relating to an application for approval of powers exercised under an emergency authorisation;

any information that is likely to enable the identification of a person, object or premises specified in a warrant, an emergency authorisation or a tracking device authorisation; and any information obtained by an officer of WA Police Force without the authority of a warrant, a tracking device authorisation or an emergency authorisation that was subsequently approved, in contravention of the requirement for such a warrant, tracking device authorisation or emergency authorisation.
sections.b: Protected information is defined as: any information obtained from the use of a surveillance device under a warrant, an emergency authorisation or a tracking device authorisation, any information relating to an application for, the issue of, the existence of, or the expiration of, a warrant, an emergency authorisation or a tracking device authorisation; any information relating to an application for approval of powers exercised under an emergency authorisation; any information that is likely to enable the identification of a person, object or premises specified in a warrant, an emergency authorisation or a tracking device authorisation; and any information obtained by an officer of WA Police Force without the authority of a warrant, a tracking device authorisation or an emergency authorisation that was subsequently approved, in contravention of the requirement for such a warrant, tracking device authorisation or emergency authorisation.

sections.h:
Any use, recording, communication or publication of protected information must be in compliance with the Act. Protected information may be used, recorded, communicated or published, or may be admitted in evidence, if it is necessary to do so for any of the following purposes:

the investigation of a relevant offence (as defined by the Act);

the making of a decision whether or not to bring a prosecution for a relevant offence;

a relevant proceeding;

an investigation of a complaint against, or into the conduct of, a public officer (as defined by the Act); and inspection, compliance and reporting purposes required under the Act.

The above list is not exhaustive and investigators and other officers concerned should direct any enquiries to the OIC of SOAU.
sections.b: Any use, recording, communication or publication of protected information must be in compliance with the Act. Protected information may be used, recorded, communicated or published, or may be admitted in evidence, if it is necessary to do so for any of the following purposes: the investigation of a relevant offence (as defined by the Act); the making of a decision whether or not to bring a prosecution for a relevant offence; a relevant proceeding; an investigation of a complaint against, or into the conduct of, a public officer (as defined by the Act); and inspection, compliance and reporting purposes required under the Act. The above list is not exhaustive and investigators and other officers concerned should direct any enquiries to the OIC of SOAU.

sections.t: Communicating Protected Information to Other Law Enforcement Agencies
sections.h:
The Act contains provisions for the communication of protected information obtained through the use of surveillance devices with other law enforcement agencies. Details of this communication must be entered onto a Register maintained by SOAU, which is subject to inspection by oversight bodies. Therefore, it is the policy of SOAU not to permit communication of protected information obtained through the use of surveillance devices with other law enforcement agencies without the permission of the OIC of SOAU.

Investigating officers and other officers concerned who wish to communicate protected information to any officer of a law enforcement agency other than WA Police Force are required to complete and submit a Communication of Protected Information form to the OIC of SOAU for consideration. This Communication of Protected Information form is to filed in the relevant Codafile, once granted or refused by the OIC of SOAU.
sections.b: The Act contains provisions for the communication of protected information obtained through the use of surveillance devices with other law enforcement agencies. Details of this communication must be entered onto a Register maintained by SOAU, which is subject to inspection by oversight bodies. Therefore, it is the policy of SOAU not to permit communication of protected information obtained through the use of surveillance devices with other law enforcement agencies without the permission of the OIC of SOAU. Investigating officers and other officers concerned who wish to communicate protected information to any officer of a law enforcement agency other than WA Police Force are required to complete and submit a Communication of Protected Information form to the OIC of SOAU for consideration. This Communication of Protected Information form is to filed in the relevant Codafile, once granted or refused by the OIC of SOAU.

sections.t: Record Keeping of the Use of Protected Information
sections.h:
Pursuant to section 52(1)(e) to (h) of the Act, the Chief Officer is required to record the following details concerning the use of protected information:

details of each use by WA Police Force, or by an officer of WA Police Force, of information obtained by the use of a surveillance device by an officer of WA Police Force;

details of each communication by an officer of WA Police Force to a person other than an officer of WA Police Force of information obtained by the use of a surveillance device by an officer of WA Police Force;

details of each occasion when information obtained by the use of a surveillance device by an officer of WA Police Force was given in evidence in a relevant proceeding; and details of each occasion when information obtained by the use of a surveillance device by an officer of WA Police Force was used in the location and safe recovery of a child to whom a recovery order related.
sections.b: Pursuant to section 52(1)(e) to (h) of the Act, the Chief Officer is required to record the following details concerning the use of protected information: details of each use by WA Police Force, or by an officer of WA Police Force, of information obtained by the use of a surveillance device by an officer of WA Police Force; details of each communication by an officer of WA Police Force to a person other than an officer of WA Police Force of information obtained by the use of a surveillance device by an officer of WA Police Force; details of each occasion when information obtained by the use of a surveillance device by an officer of WA Police Force was given in evidence in a relevant proceeding; and details of each occasion when information obtained by the use of a surveillance device by an officer of WA Police Force was used in the location and safe recovery of a child to whom a recovery order related.

sections.h:
All access to electronic documentation that relates to protected information is only to be made available to those people entitled to deal with the protected information. Access to the protected information must be in accordance with WA Police Force policy LO-01.06 Restricted Access to Information on the Police Computer System, in addition to compliance with Part 6 Division 1 of the Act, concerning restrictions on use, communication and publication of information.

All access to hard copy documentation that relates to protected information is only to be made available to those people entitled to deal with the protected information. Most hard copy records in relation to protected information will be stored in the secure environment of SOAU, however investigators and other officers concerned must ensure any hard copy documentation that relates to protected information, regardless of its location, is stored in a secure-access area and access is restricted to those people entitled to deal with the protected information.
sections.b: All access to electronic documentation that relates to protected information is only to be made available to those people entitled to deal with the protected information. Access to the protected information must be in accordance with WA Police Force policy LO-01.06 Restricted Access to Information on the Police Computer System, in addition to compliance with Part 6 Division 1 of the Act, concerning restrictions on use, communication and publication of information. All access to hard copy documentation that relates to protected information is only to be made available to those people entitled to deal with the protected information. Most hard copy records in relation to protected information will be stored in the secure environment of SOAU, however investigators and other officers concerned must ensure any hard copy documentation that relates to protected information, regardless of its location, is stored in a secure-access area and access is restricted to those people entitled to deal with the protected information.

sections.t: Destruction and Retention of Protected Information:

sections.h:
Any record or report that comprises protected information is to be subject to a destruction regime pursuant to section 46(1)(b) of the Act. The Chief Officer must cause to be destroyed any record or report that comprises protected information:

as soon as practicable after the making of the record or report, if the Chief Officer is satisfied that no civil or criminal proceeding to which the material contained in the record or report relates has been, or is likely to be, commenced, and that the material contained in the record or report is not likely to be required in connection with any activity or purpose referred to in sections 45(4), 45(5) or 45A(1) of the Act; and within the period of 5 years after the making of the record or report, and within each period of 5 years thereafter, unless, before the end of that period, the Chief Officer is satisfied in relation to the material contained in the record or report of a matter referred to in the paragraph above and certifies to that effect [i.e. that the matter is ongoing].

Those records or reports that are consistent with the WA Police Force Serious Crime Exhibit Retention Management (SCERM) policy (CR-15.2 Serious Crime Exhibit Retention and Management) will be dealt with under those provisions. This is to ensure WA Police Force remains compliant under the Act, as well as consistent with the objectives of the policy, being to ensure all serious crime exhibits are managed in such a way as to protect any future loss of evidence, even in circumstances where it is thought a trial (guilty verdict) has brought a conclusion to a crime.

SOAU will maintain a Destruction Register concerning all records and reports comprising protected information and will continually reconcile the storage of records and reports comprising protected information against the requirement for destruction. The Destructions Register is to include the following information in relation to the relevant warrant, emergency authorisation or tracking device authorisation:

the warrant or authorisation designator;

the date of application;

whether the application was granted, refused or withdrawn;

the period in force;

the date of revocation or expiration;

the name of the investigating officer;

the unit the investigating officer is attached to;

the destruction review date (to be adjusted as required);

whether or not a Destruction Advice Report has been received;

details of the use of protected information;

whether the records and reports are to be destroyed or retained;

whether the records and reports are subject to the Criminal Organisation Control Act 2012 (WA);

whether the records and reports are subject to the WA Police Force SCERM policy;

whether the records and reports have been inspected by the Commonwealth Ombudsman;

whether destruction of the records and reports has been authorised by the Chief Officer;

the location of all records and reports; and

whether the records and reports have been destroyed.

The investigating officer is required to complete and submit a Destruction Advice Report concerning the above information to the OIC of SOAU with a recommendation as to whether the records and reports are to be destroyed or retained.

Once the decision to destroy records and reports comprising protected information has been approved, the destruction of said records and reports is to occur in accordance with WA Police Force policy RM 10 Records Retention and Disposal Guidelines and in consultation with the Records Management Centre, Office of Information Management.

SOAU has a dedicated destructions officer to ensure no destructions are carried out in contravention of section 46 of the Act. Any information or records required for destruction by the investigator are now forwarded to SOAU to facilitate.

All investigators are now provided with a copy of these Standard Operating Procedures which refers to the destruction process and retention of records for longer than 5 years.

Those records or reports that are consistent with the WA Police Force Serious Crime Exhibit Retention Management (SCERM) policy (CR-15.2 Serious Crime Exhibit Retention and Management) will be dealt with under those provisions. This is to ensure WA Police Force remains compliant under the Act, as well as consistent with the objectives of the policy, being to ensure all serious crime exhibits are managed in such a way as to protect any future loss of evidence, even in circumstances where it is thought a trial (guilty verdict) has brought a conclusion to a crime. SOAU will maintain a Destruction Register concerning all records and reports comprising protected information and will continually reconcile the storage of records and reports comprising protected information against the requirement for destruction. The Destructions Register is to include the following information in relation to the relevant warrant, emergency authorisation or tracking device authorisation: the warrant or authorisation designator; the date of application; whether the application was granted, refused or withdrawn; the period in force; the date of revocation or expiration; the name of the investigating officer; the unit the investigating officer is attached to; the destruction review date (to be adjusted as required); whether or not a Destruction Advice Report has been received; details of the use of protected information; whether the records and reports are to be destroyed or retained; whether the records and reports are subject to the Criminal Organisation Control Act 2012 (WA); whether the records and reports are subject to the WA Police Force SCERM policy; whether the records and reports have been inspected by the Commonwealth Ombudsman; whether destruction of the records and reports has been authorised by the Chief Officer; the location of all records and reports; and whether the records and reports have been destroyed. The investigating officer is required to complete and submit a Destruction Advice Report concerning the above information to the OIC of SOAU with a recommendation as to whether the records and reports are to be destroyed or retained. Once the decision to destroy records and reports comprising protected information has been approved, the destruction of said records and reports is to occur in accordance with WA Police Force policy RM 10 Records Retention and Disposal Guidelines and in consultation with the Records Management Centre, Office of Information Management. Any information or records required for destruction by the investigator are now forwarded to SOAU to facilitate. All investigators are now provided with a copy of these Standard Operating Procedures which refers to the destruction process and retention of records for longer than 5 years.

sections.t: Instrument of Delegation Section 63 of the Act
C O N F I D E N T I A L
sections.h:
Officer in Charge

Special Operations Application Unit

6th Floor, 60 Beaufort Street

Perth, W.A. 6000

SURVEILLANCE DEVICES ACT 2004 (Commonwealth) - SECT 49 EXAMPLE Report on each warrant or authorisation

This report refers to the following Surveillance Devices Act (2004) (Commonwealth) warrant or authorisation:

Operation: OPERATION NAME

Warrant/Authorisation Number: WARRANT NUMBER

Applicant: APPLICANT LISTED ON THE WARRANT

(2) In the case of a surveillance device warrant, or an emergency authorisation for the use of a surveillance device, or a tracking device authorisation, the report must:

(a) state whether the warrant or authorisation was executed; YES OR NO, IF NO, WAS THE WARRANT REVOKED AND IF NOT PLEASE STATE THE REASON

(b) if so:

(i) state the name of the person primarily responsible for the execution of the warrant or authorisation; SELF EXPLANATORY (SE)

(ii) state the name of each person involved in the installation, maintenance or retrieval of the surveillance device; IF APPLICABLE PLEASE OBTAIN DETAILS FROM POLICE TECHNICAL UNIT INCLUDING OBTAINING ANY RELEVANT RUNNING SHEETS, TIMES, DATES, ETC OF EACH INSTALLATION, MAINTENANCE INCLUDING ANY ATTEMPTS MADE.

(iii) state the kind of surveillance device used; LISTENING, OPTICAL, TRACKING AND/OR DATA

(iv) state the period during which the device was used; INCLUDE EACH TIME THE SD WAS DEPLOYED, INCLUDING TIMES, DATES, LOCATIONS (PLEASE PROVIDE RUNNING SHEETS)

(v) state the name, if known, of any person whose conversations or activities were overheard, recorded, monitored, listened to or observed by the use of the device; REFER TO RELEVANT TIMES AND DATES LISTED AT POINT (iv). PLEASE DESCRIBE THE DEVICE/EQUIPMENT THE MATERIAL WAS RECORDED ON, DESCRIBE THE RELEVANT SECURITY AND ENCRYPTION.

(vi) state the name, if known, of any person whose location was determined by the use of a tracking device; REFER TO RELEVANT LOCATIONS, TIMES AND DATES LISTED AT POINT (iv)

(vii) give details of any premises on which the device was installed or any place at which the device was used; SE

(viii) give details of any object in or on which the device was installed and any premises where the object was located when the device was installed; SE

(ix) if the warrant is issued or the authorisation given in respect of the investigation of a relevant offence--give details of the benefit to the investigation of the use of the device and of the general use made or to be made of any evidence or information obtained by the use of the device; SE

(x) if the warrant is issued or the authorisation given in respect of the location and safe recovery of a child to whom a recovery order relates--give details of use of the device in assisting with the location and safe recovery of the child; INCLUDE EACH TIME THE SD WAS DEPLOYED, INCLUDING TIMES, DATES, LOCATIONS (PLEASE PROVIDE RUNNING SHEETS

(xa) if the warrant is issued or the authorisation given for the purposes of an integrity operation--give details of the benefit to the operation of the use of the device and of the general use made or to be made of any evidence or information obtained by the use of the device; SE

(xb) if the warrant is a control order warrant--give the details specified in subsection (2A); NOT APPLICABLE

(xi) give details of the communication of evidence or information obtained by the use of the device to persons other than officers of the agency; IF YES PLEASE PROVIDE DETAILS, NOTES, RUNNING SHEETS ETC.

(xii) give details of the compliance with the conditions (if any) to which the warrant or authorisation was subject; GIVE DETAILS IF APPLICABLE

(c) if the warrant or authorisation was extended or varied, state:

(i) the number of extensions or variations; SE

(ii) the reasons for them. GIVE DETAILS

(2A) For the purposes of subparagraph (2)(b)(xb), the details are:

(a) the benefit of the use of the device in: NA

(i) protecting the public from a terrorist act; NA

(ii) preventing the provision of support for, or the facilitation of, a terrorist act; NA

(iii) preventing the provision of support for, or the facilitation of, the engagement in a hostile activity in a foreign country; NA

(iv) determining whether a control order has been, or is being, complied with; NA

(b) the general use to be made of any evidence or information obtained by the use of the device. NA

(2B) In the case of a computer access warrant, or an emergency authorisation, for access to data held in a computer, the report must:

(a) state whether the warrant or authorisation was executed; YES OR NO, IF NO, WAS THE WARRANT REVOKED AND IF NOT PLEASE STATE THE REASON

(b) if so:

(i) state the name of the person primarily responsible for the execution of the warrant or authorisation; SE

(ii) state the name of each person involved in accessing data under the warrant or authorisation; SE

(iii) state the period during which the data was accessed; PLEASE PROVIDE TIMES, DATES AND RUNNING SHEETS

(iv) state the name, if known, of any person whose data was accessed; IF APPLICABLE PLEASE OBTAIN DETAILS FROM COMPUTER CRIME, POLICE TECHNICAL UNIT OR SIMILIAR INCLUDING OBTAINING ANY RELEVANT RUNNING SHEETS, TIMES, DATES, ETC

(v) give details of any premises at which the computer was located; REFER TO RELEVANT LOCATIONS, TIMES AND DATES

(vi) if the warrant is issued, or the authorisation is given, in respect of the investigation of a relevant offence--give details of the benefit to the investigation of the accessed data and of the general use made, or to be made, of any evidence or information obtained by the access to data; SE

(vii) if the warrant is issued, or the authorisation is given, in respect of the location and safe recovery of a child to whom a recovery order relates--give details of the use of the accessed data in assisting with the location and safe recovery of the child; SE

(viii) if the warrant is issued, or the authorisation is given, for the purposes of an integrity operation--give details of the benefit to the operation of the accessed data and of the general use made, or to be made, of any evidence or information obtained by the access to data; SE

(ix) if the warrant is a control order access warrant--give the details specified in subsection (2C); NA, WA Police Force DOES NOT APPLY FOR CONTROL ORDER WARRANTS

(x) give details of the communication of evidence or information obtained by access to data held in the computer to persons other than officers of the agency; NA

(xi) give details of the compliance with the conditions (if any) to which the warrant or authorisation was subject; NA

(c) if the warrant or authorisation was extended or varied, state:

(i) the number of extensions or variations; NA

(ii) the reasons for them. NA

(2C) For the purposes of subparagraph (2B)(b)(ix), the details are:

(a) the benefit of obtaining access to data held in the computer in:

(i) protecting the public from a terrorist act; NA

(ii) preventing the provision of support for, or the facilitation of, a terrorist act; NA

(iii) preventing the provision of support for, or the facilitation of, the engagement in a hostile activity in a foreign country; NA

(iv) determining whether a control order has been, or is being, complied with; NA

(b) the general use to be made of any evidence or information obtained by access to data held in the computer. NA

(3) In the case of a retrieval warrant, the report must:

(a) give details of any premises entered, anything opened and any object removed and replaced under the warrant; PLEASE PROVIDE TIMES, DATES AND RUNNING SHEETS

(b) state whether the surveillance device was retrieved under the warrant; PLEASE PROVIDE TIMES, DATES AND RUNNING SHEETS

(c) if the device was not retrieved, state the reason why; IF NO, WAS THE WARRANT REVOKED AND IF NOT PLEASE STATE THE REASON

(d) give details of the compliance with the conditions (if any) to which the warrant was subject. SE

Forwarded for information and reporting.

sections.h:
Commonwealth SDA (Warrant approved/issued)

Your application for a Commonwealth SDA warrant(s)/or extension to warrant XX of 202X, has gone to court and a warrant(s) was issued/or extension granted by [TITLE – Justice] XXXXX for a period of XX days.

Please note the EXPIRY DATE of XX/XX/202X.

It is your responsibility to submit a supplementary TRF to the State Intelligence and command Portfolio, Covert Coordinator 14 days prior to the expiry date if you believe that you are going to require an extension/further extension.

Please liaise with the Police Technical Unit, Operations Manager on 08 9315 4409 (all hours) to arrange the installation or retrieval of surveillance devices pursuant to your warrant (s).

As the applicant for a Commonwealth SDA warrant you must be aware and abide by the following legislation compliance matters:

For assistance on Commonwealth SDA procedures seek further information Blue Book / State Crime / Crime Services and Services / Special Operations Application Unit (SOAU) / Surveillance Devices Storage of Protected Information:

Section 46(1)(a) of the Act requires the chief officer to ensure that every record comprising protected information is kept in a secure place that is not accessible to people who are not entitled to deal with the information. The definition of 'protected information' under s 44(1) of the Act includes information obtained from the use of a surveillance device.

PLEASE ENSURE THAT ANY STORAGE OF PRODUCT ON USB’s, HARD DRIVES OR DISCS ARE ENCRYPTED AND ONLY AVAILABLE TO THOSE PERSON ENTITLED TO DEAL WITH IT.

Keeping Records of Each use:

Under s 45 of the Act, the use, recording, communication, or publication of protected information is prohibited unless it is for a permitted purpose detailed under s 45(4) of the Act. Section 45(5) of the Act further provides permitted purposes to use, record, communicate or publish, or admit in evidence protected information.

Under s 52(1)(e) of the Act, the chief officer of a law enforcement agency must keep 'details of each use' by the agency, or by a law enforcement officer of the agency, of information obtained using a surveillance device or access to data held in a computer. Section 52(1)(f) of the Act requires the same for ‘details of each communication' by a law enforcement officer of the agency to a person other than a law enforcement officer of the agency.

PLEASE ENSURE RECORDS ARE KEPT ON EACH USE AND THE RECORD KEEPING REQUIREMENTS SURROUNDING EACH USE. PLEASE KEEP DETAILED RUNNING SHEETS ON EACH USE OF THE SDA AND SUPPLY A COPY OF THESE RUNNING SHEETS TO BE KEPT ON FILE.

Revocation of Warrants:

The chief officer must revoke a surveillance device warrant, by instrument in writing, if the prescribed circumstances in s 21 of the Act apply. This includes where the chief officer is satisfied that the use of a surveillance device under the warrant is no longer necessary for the purpose of enabling evidence to be obtained of the commission of a relevant offence or the identity or location of an offender.

PLEASE NOTIFY SOAU IMMEDIATELY IF THE WAARANT IS NO LONGER NECESSARY FOR ITS ORIGINAL PURPOSE. SOAU WILL THEN ARRANGE REVOCATION WITH THE CHIEF OFFICER.

Final Investigators Report:

Section 49(1) of the Act states that the chief officer must as soon as practicable after a warrant or authority ceases to be in force, make a report to the Minister.

PLEASE REFERENCE RECORDS KEPT FOR THE USE OF THE SDA INCLUDING ANY RUNNING SHEETS WHEN COMPILING THE INVESTIGATOR REPORT.

For assistance in completing this report please refer to example report situated at Blue Book / State Crime / Crime Support and Services / Special Operations Application Unit / Commonwealth SDA Procedures NOTE - If you are making an application for any covert services, either for a telecommunication intercept, surveillance devices, physical surveillance or undercover officers you are required to complete a 'WAPOL Tasking Request Form’ Please contact the SOAU Covert Applications Supervisor on 9223522 if you have any queries or concerns.

Destruction of Protected Information:

Destruction of Protected Information and/or data obtained through the execution of surveillance device warrant will be undertaken in accordance to a destruction regime pursuant to section 46(1)(b) of the Act.

Only upon issue of a Destruction Order is Protected Information to be destroyed.

Inquiries regarding the destruction of Protected Information should be directed to SOAU.

Any Protected Information obtained through execution of a surveillance warrant held by another law enforcement or government agency shall be subject to the WA Police Force Destruction Process with evidence of the destruction required to be recorded within the warrant file.

SOAU will manage and coordinate the destruction of all protected information on behalf of the WA Police Force.

INSTRUMENT TO AUTHORISE ANOTHER LAW ENFORCEMENT OFFICER AS PRIMARILY RESPONSIBLE FOR THE EXECUTION OF A WARRANT OR AUTHORISATION

sections.h: Pursuant to section 46(1)(b) of the Surveillance Devices Act 2004 (Commonwealth) (the Act) any record or report (including optical discs, electronic product and related material) that comprises protected information, as defined in the Act, is to be subject to a destruction regime. The Investigating Officer is to complete the Destruction Advice Report below and submit it to Special Operations Application Unit in order to facilitate the destruction process.

sections.h:
This form is to be completed by any Investigating Officer who wishes to communicate protected information, as defined by section 45 of the Surveillance Devices Act 2004 (Commonwealth) (the Act), to any officer of a law enforcement agency other than Western Australia Police.

It is the policy of Special Operations Application Unit (SOAU) not to permit communication of protected information obtained through the use of surveillance devices with other law enforcement agencies without the permission of the Officer-in-Charge of SOAU. The use, communication and publication of protected information is restricted under the Act and is subject to record keeping requirements if communication of the protected information is approved by the OIC SOAU.

The Investigating Officer is to complete the below form and submit to SOAU for approval.

(if YES, it is the Investigating Officer’s responsibility to liaise with Special Operations Application Unit regarding further use of the protected information) Since the Investigator Report has been completed, has there been any further arrests or prosecutions that are either wholly or partly based on the protected information obtained by the use of the surveillance device? (if YES, list names, charges and dates of arrest) Are there any court proceedings or asset forfeiture hearings currently under, or likely to be underway, which require records and reports comprising protected information to be kept? sections.h:
Section 45(7) of the Surveillance Devices Act 2004 (Commonwealth) If protected information obtained through the use of a surveillance device by a law enforcement officer of a particular law enforcement agency (the originating agency):

is communicated to another law enforcement agency (by communicating it to the chief officer or another officer of that agency); or is communicated to any agency that is not a law enforcement agency (other than the Australian Security Intelligence Organisation and the agencies within the meaning of the Intelligence Services Act 2001) (by communicating it to the officer in charge of that agency or to another officer of that agency);

for a particular purpose, the protected information that has been so communicated:

may be communicated from one officer to another within that agency or organisation for that purpose only; and must not, except for the purpose of bringing a relevant proceeding, or a State or Territory relevant proceeding, be communicated to any person who is not a member of that agency or organisation.
sections.b: Section 45(7) of the Surveillance Devices Act 2004 (Commonwealth) If protected information obtained through the use of a surveillance device by a law enforcement officer of a particular law enforcement agency (the originating agency): is communicated to another law enforcement agency (by communicating it to the chief officer or another officer of that agency); or is communicated to any agency that is not a law enforcement agency (other than the Australian Security Intelligence Organisation and the agencies within the meaning of the Intelligence Services Act 2001) (by communicating it to the officer in charge of that agency or to another officer of that agency); for a particular purpose, the protected information that has been so communicated: may be communicated from one officer to another within that agency or organisation for that purpose only; and must not, except for the purpose of bringing a relevant proceeding, or a State or Territory relevant proceeding, be communicated to any person who is not a member of that agency or organisation.
